title: Channel Manager (UK)
descriptionhtml:
About The Role

ThreatAware is hiring its first dedicated Channel Account Manager in the UK — a high-impact role at the heart of our partner led growth strategy. Reporting directly to the Head of Channel, you'll own and develop relationships with our key Value-Added Resellers (VARs) and security partners, turning those partnerships into a consistent and growing pipeline of new business.

This isn't a role for someone who wants to stay close to the office. You'll be out in the field; attending joint marketing events, building relationships with partner Account Managers, and embedding ThreatAware within the sales motions of some of the UK's most influential resellers.

You'll also have the opportunity to explore new market opportunities, helping us expand our reach across the UK security ecosystem.

Why ThreatAware?

ThreatAware gives security teams a single source of truth for every device and tool in their organisation. We deploy in under 30 minutes, with 150+ integrations and no agents required. On average, we reveal that 10% of devices on a network go completely undetected and 30% of security controls are missing or silently failing.

The platform complements rather than competes with the security tools already in a partner's portfolio — giving their clients visibility into whether those tools are deployed, working, and covering every device. It's a natural, non-disruptive conversation for partners to have, and a logical addition to any security stack they're already recommending.

Now we're building something bigger. In February 2026, we raised $25M from One Peak, now we're scaling our North American operations and launching our AI-powered security workspace, giving security teams the freedom to build exactly the tools and automations they need, powered by six years of the most accurate cyber asset data in the market.

What You'll Do

  • Own and grow our VAR relationships, building deep connections across their Account Manager populations to generate a consistent flow of qualified opportunities.
  • Develop and nurture our specialist security partner relationships, helping them confidently position and sell ThreatAware to their clients.
  • Be a constant, trusted presence with partner teams; attending joint marketing events, co-selling on opportunities, and making ThreatAware the go-to recommendation when a partner's client has a cyber asset visibility challenge.
  • Prospect for new VAR and reseller partnerships that can open up fresh market opportunities for ThreatAware in the UK.
  • Support partners with the materials and knowledge they need to sell effectively — working closely with marketing and product to ensure our channel collateral is sharp and up to date.
  • Report on pipeline health, partner engagement, and channel-sourced revenue, feeding accurate forecasts to the Head of Channel.
  • Work closely with ThreatAware's internal Account Executive team to align on joint opportunities, ensure smooth handoffs and maximise the impact of partner-sourced pipeline.

What We're Looking For

  • Minimum 4 years of channel or partner sales experience, ideally within cyber security or enterprise software.
  • A solid understanding of the VAR and reseller ecosystem; you know how partners operate, what motivates their AMs, and how to build influence within a reseller organisation.
  • Experience managing or supporting complex, technical products that require education and enablement at the partner level.
  • A track record of building pipeline through partners, not just managing existing relationships.
  • Commercially astute; you understand margin, deal structure, and what makes a partnership work for both sides.
  • Relationship driven, you enjoy spending time with partner teams, not just emailing them.
  • Autonomous and self-directed — you can prioritise effectively and don't need to be told where to focus.

title: Regional Sales Manager
descriptionhtml:
About Charlotte Tilbury Beauty

Founded by British makeup artist and beauty entrepreneur Charlotte Tilbury MBE in 2013, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has revolutionised the face of the global beauty industry by de-coding makeup applications for everyone, everywhere, with an easy-to-use, easy-to-choose, easy-to-gift range. Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty continues to break records across countries, channels, and categories and to scale at pace.

Over the last 10 years, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has experienced exceptional growth and is one of the most talked about brands in the beauty industry and beyond. It has become a global sensation across 50 markets (and growing), with over 2,300 employees globally who are part of the Dream Team making the magic happen.

Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty is a truly global business, delivering market-leading growth, innovative retail and product launches fuelled by industry-leading tech — all with an internal culture of embracing challenges, disruptive thinking, winning together, and sharing the magic. The energy behind the brand is infectious, and as we grow, we are always looking for extraordinary talent who want to be part of this our success and help drive our limitless ambitions.

About The Role

The Regional Sales Manager role is responsible for leading a defined group of retailers/areas within a regional market, to deliver market leading retail sales and growth by engaging and leading the Field Sales Manager population/ Flagship Manager population. The role is responsible for clear and consistent messaging across the entire region, enabling the sharing of best practise and reporting, analysing and reacting to immediate trade risks and opportunities, Whilst managing the inflating and deflating of costs in line with trading patterns, and having overall accountability of the sales, profitability and productivity of their region. They will strive to have consistency across retailers in all Key Performance Indicators including{{{{:}}}} footfall and conversion, average transaction value, customer experience, data capture, social advocacy and eNPS scores both in the air and on the ground.

*Please note - This role can be based anywhere within the UK. You will be comfortable with occasional travel to the London HQ, typically the role will be work from home Monday & Friday with store visits in between.*

Role Accountabilities

Commercial Leadership{{{{:}}}}

Deliver retail sales in line with forecast commitments, achieving total retail sales across the region, Ensuring optimum and consistent KPI performance (as set out within the REOP strategy)

Work with the SNAM on store level forecasts ,and align on associated targets with the Retail Sales and Operations Manager

Ensure robust action is being taken week on week, and at mid-week to optimise sales performance with the Field Sales Managers

Deliver reporting for the region that can be dissected easily and is presentable to Senior Stakeholders

Optimise ways of working alongside the Senior Training Managers. between the FTM and FSM population to enable swift reaction to trade whether that be course correcting or sharing best practise

High Performance Team

Lead and manage the team of Field Sales Managers, ensuring each manager has clear objectives in line with the sprint process

Work with the Retail People Partner of the region to support you in the delivery of your retail people plan.

Lead initiatives and actively encourage action plans to address engagement needs identified from Peakon, building a culture of inclusion and motivation within your defined region, enabling consistency within the retail teams.

Work alongside the Senior Training Managers to ensure commercial and development training needs are met within the region

To analyse HR stats and labour turnover for the areas within your region and identify and action plan with the FSM any off track areas, including consistency of ER and performance management

To ensure that artist deployment and contract spread, and productivity are optimised within your region and are being addressed

To own team engagement in the region by holding listening groups at least bi-annually with appropriate action taken, or fed back to the relevant stakeholder

To make recommendations to the Retail Sales and Operations Manager on how to further drive engagement, optimise productivity and ensure operational excellence

Retail Sales Strategy

Taking the Retail sales and operations strategy define the strategy for the regional retailers, and work with the FSMs to ensure this is distilled into individual store strategy

Work in close collaboration with the Senior Training Managers to develop a launch and sustain plans for all stores and NPD, to ensure all Charlotte Tilbury Beauty stores reach #1 ranking across all partner department stores

Review the strategy regularly in the moment to ensure that it is being delivered and feeding back any barriers/ shortfalls where support is required

Where applicable, ensure there is differentiation of the department store concession, free standing stores, and Specialty model, contributing to the marketing and service initiatives in both environments, based on customer and store insight; work closely with the Pro Artistry team to support the creation of artistry centres of excellence in our flagship stores and a high level of artistry in all

Identify and capitalise on opportunities to drive brand awareness, performance, and market share across the region, with a particular focus on consistency across areas

Set standards across the estate for executional excellence, supporting the development and embedding of new processes and working cross-functionally to improve brand presentation and NPD moments in your areas

Work with the Retail Operations Team to ensure communication with the field is effective and timely and feedback fluidly where improvements can be made

Always driving the omnichannel sales number where there is no conflict of interest with retail partners, and Commercial social advocacy initiatives where applicable

Have good relationships with all Retailer Store Managers, and ensure the FSMs are optimising their department manager relationships, to optimise brand presence and realise incremental opportunities at store level

Effective Cost Optimisation

Distribute and control retail staffing costs via the FSMs, and provide recommendations to the Retail Sales and Operations Manager where staffing needs to be flexed up or down depending on trade patterns, and present the relevant business case outside of the forecast cycle. Ensure in store retail partners are aligned to cost share, and written evidence is obtained.

Review recruitment stats for your region and progress with the recruitment team weekly

Sign off and submit to finance your regions commission files, informing the Retail Sales and Operations Manager of any discrepancies

Ensure the FSMs are sticking to their T and E budgets, and take the appropriate action

Reporting Relationships

Reporting to the Head of Retail sales

Direct reports of Field Sales Managers and Flagship store managers

About You

Able to travel within the region - UK

Strong field management or multi‑site experience with a track record of developing talent and driving growth

Strong people focus and passion for team development

Target‑driven with strong numerical and analytical skills

Confident communicator with excellent presentation skills

Personable, approachable, and able to build rapport quickly

Strong influencing skills

Self‑starter Who Can Motivate Themselves And Others

Comfortable with ambiguity and solution‑oriented

Highly organised with the ability to manage and adapt to multiple priorities

Charlotte Tilbury is a fast-paced and dynamic environment where nimble mindsets, striving to deliver the best and wanting to be part of a global #dreaamteam are key. Even though we have requirements, our experience and background are just a guide, we still love to welcome applicants with more or less experience stated, provided necessary skills can be demonstrated.

At Charlotte Tilbury Beauty, our mission is to empower everybody in the world to be the most beautiful version of themselves. We celebrate and support this by encouraging and hiring people with diverse backgrounds, cultures, voices, beliefs, and perspectives into our growing global workforce. By doing so, we better serve our communities, customers, employees - and the candidates that take part in our recruitment process.

If you want to learn more about life at Charlotte Tilbury Beauty please follow our LinkedIn page!

Benefits

Why join us?

  • We offer a structured induction programme - Charlotte's Magic Academy. This exclusive and bespoke induction trains on all aspects of the role that you will need to succeed in providing red carpet customer experience with follow up training provided during your first months of employment. Focusing on artistry and commerce, you will gain a full understanding of our artistry and service expectations
  • Structured career development - our team of industry experts are here to support and work with you to explore your learning potential and career goals
  • You receive exclusive launches of our incredible products before anyone else - not only that, but you even receive a full Charlotte Tilbury Look on completion of Charlotte's Magic Academy so you can be a true brand advocate
  • Other fabulous benefits such as birthdays off work, team sales incentives and many more

description_html:
About Charlotte Tilbury Beauty

Founded by British makeup artist and beauty entrepreneur Charlotte Tilbury MBE in 2013, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has revolutionised the face of the global beauty industry by de-coding makeup applications for everyone, everywhere, with an easy-to-use, easy-to-choose, easy-to-gift range. Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty continues to break records across countries, channels, and categories and to scale at pace.

Over the last 10 years, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty has experienced exceptional growth and is one of the most talked about brands in the beauty industry and beyond. It has become a global sensation across 50 markets (and growing), with over 2,300 employees globally who are part of the Dream Team making the magic happen.

Today, Charlotte Tilbury Beauty is a truly global business, delivering market-leading growth, innovative retail and product launches fuelled by industry-leading tech — all with an internal culture of embracing challenges, disruptive thinking, winning together, and sharing the magic. The energy behind the brand is infectious, and as we grow, we are always looking for extraordinary talent who want to be part of this our success and help drive our limitless ambitions.

About The Role

The Regional Sales Manager role is responsible for leading a defined group of retailers/areas within a regional market, to deliver market leading retail sales and growth by engaging and leading the Field Sales Manager population/ Flagship Manager population. The role is responsible for clear and consistent messaging across the entire region, enabling the sharing of best practise and reporting, analysing and reacting to immediate trade risks and opportunities, Whilst managing the inflating and deflating of costs in line with trading patterns, and having overall accountability of the sales, profitability and productivity of their region. They will strive to have consistency across retailers in all Key Performance Indicators including{{{{:}}}} footfall and conversion, average transaction value, customer experience, data capture, social advocacy and eNPS scores both in the air and on the ground.

*Please note - This role can be based anywhere within the UK. You will be comfortable with occasional travel to the London HQ, typically the role will be work from home Monday & Friday with store visits in between.*

Role Accountabilities

Commercial Leadership{{{{:}}}}

Deliver retail sales in line with forecast commitments, achieving total retail sales across the region, Ensuring optimum and consistent KPI performance (as set out within the REOP strategy)

Work with the SNAM on store level forecasts ,and align on associated targets with the Retail Sales and Operations Manager

Ensure robust action is being taken week on week, and at mid-week to optimise sales performance with the Field Sales Managers

Deliver reporting for the region that can be dissected easily and is presentable to Senior Stakeholders

Optimise ways of working alongside the Senior Training Managers. between the FTM and FSM population to enable swift reaction to trade whether that be course correcting or sharing best practise

High Performance Team

Lead and manage the team of Field Sales Managers, ensuring each manager has clear objectives in line with the sprint process

Work with the Retail People Partner of the region to support you in the delivery of your retail people plan.

Lead initiatives and actively encourage action plans to address engagement needs identified from Peakon, building a culture of inclusion and motivation within your defined region, enabling consistency within the retail teams.

Work alongside the Senior Training Managers to ensure commercial and development training needs are met within the region

To analyse HR stats and labour turnover for the areas within your region and identify and action plan with the FSM any off track areas, including consistency of ER and performance management

To ensure that artist deployment and contract spread, and productivity are optimised within your region and are being addressed

To own team engagement in the region by holding listening groups at least bi-annually with appropriate action taken, or fed back to the relevant stakeholder

To make recommendations to the Retail Sales and Operations Manager on how to further drive engagement, optimise productivity and ensure operational excellence

Retail Sales Strategy

Taking the Retail sales and operations strategy define the strategy for the regional retailers, and work with the FSMs to ensure this is distilled into individual store strategy

Work in close collaboration with the Senior Training Managers to develop a launch and sustain plans for all stores and NPD, to ensure all Charlotte Tilbury Beauty stores reach #1 ranking across all partner department stores

Review the strategy regularly in the moment to ensure that it is being delivered and feeding back any barriers/ shortfalls where support is required

Where applicable, ensure there is differentiation of the department store concession, free standing stores, and Specialty model, contributing to the marketing and service initiatives in both environments, based on customer and store insight; work closely with the Pro Artistry team to support the creation of artistry centres of excellence in our flagship stores and a high level of artistry in all

Identify and capitalise on opportunities to drive brand awareness, performance, and market share across the region, with a particular focus on consistency across areas

Set standards across the estate for executional excellence, supporting the development and embedding of new processes and working cross-functionally to improve brand presentation and NPD moments in your areas

Work with the Retail Operations Team to ensure communication with the field is effective and timely and feedback fluidly where improvements can be made

Always driving the omnichannel sales number where there is no conflict of interest with retail partners, and Commercial social advocacy initiatives where applicable

Have good relationships with all Retailer Store Managers, and ensure the FSMs are optimising their department manager relationships, to optimise brand presence and realise incremental opportunities at store level

Effective Cost Optimisation

Distribute and control retail staffing costs via the FSMs, and provide recommendations to the Retail Sales and Operations Manager where staffing needs to be flexed up or down depending on trade patterns, and present the relevant business case outside of the forecast cycle. Ensure in store retail partners are aligned to cost share, and written evidence is obtained.

Review recruitment stats for your region and progress with the recruitment team weekly

Sign off and submit to finance your regions commission files, informing the Retail Sales and Operations Manager of any discrepancies

Ensure the FSMs are sticking to their T and E budgets, and take the appropriate action

Reporting Relationships

Reporting to the Head of Retail sales

Direct reports of Field Sales Managers and Flagship store managers

About You

Able to travel within the region - UK

Strong field management or multi‑site experience with a track record of developing talent and driving growth

Strong people focus and passion for team development

Target‑driven with strong numerical and analytical skills

Confident communicator with excellent presentation skills

Personable, approachable, and able to build rapport quickly

Strong influencing skills

Self‑starter Who Can Motivate Themselves And Others

Comfortable with ambiguity and solution‑oriented

Highly organised with the ability to manage and adapt to multiple priorities

Charlotte Tilbury is a fast-paced and dynamic environment where nimble mindsets, striving to deliver the best and wanting to be part of a global #dreaamteam are key. Even though we have requirements, our experience and background are just a guide, we still love to welcome applicants with more or less experience stated, provided necessary skills can be demonstrated.

At Charlotte Tilbury Beauty, our mission is to empower everybody in the world to be the most beautiful version of themselves. We celebrate and support this by encouraging and hiring people with diverse backgrounds, cultures, voices, beliefs, and perspectives into our growing global workforce. By doing so, we better serve our communities, customers, employees - and the candidates that take part in our recruitment process.

If you want to learn more about life at Charlotte Tilbury Beauty please follow our LinkedIn page!

Benefits

Why join us?

  • We offer a structured induction programme - Charlotte's Magic Academy. This exclusive and bespoke induction trains on all aspects of the role that you will need to succeed in providing red carpet customer experience with follow up training provided during your first months of employment. Focusing on artistry and commerce, you will gain a full understanding of our artistry and service expectations
  • Structured career development - our team of industry experts are here to support and work with you to explore your learning potential and career goals
  • You receive exclusive launches of our incredible products before anyone else - not only that, but you even receive a full Charlotte Tilbury Look on completion of Charlotte's Magic Academy so you can be a true brand advocate
  • Other fabulous benefits such as birthdays off work, team sales incentives and many more
reason: This role focuses on retail sales management rather than B2B sales.

title: Channel Sales Manager, UK
descriptionhtml:
Who Are We?

Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.

The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.

P.S: We highly recommend reading The "API-First World" graphic novel to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.

About The Team

The Channel Sales team at Postman is responsible for scaling our enterprise business through strategic partners, including systems integrators, resellers, and technology alliances. We work cross-functionally with Sales, Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Success to drive partner-led demand, accelerate enterprise adoption, and expand Postman’s footprint within large organizations.

The Opportunity

As a Channel Sales Manager, you will play a critical role in accelerating Postman Enterprise adoption by developing and executing partner-led sales motions. This role is ideal for a seller who thrives in complex, multi-stakeholder environment

What You’ll Do

  • Own and develop a defined territory by recruiting, enrolling, and activating strategic partners (SIs, resellers, and technology partners) to drive Postman Enterprise sales revenue
  • Build and scale a partner-sourced and partner-influenced pipeline by enabling partners to identify, qualify, and progress opportunities within accounts with large Postman user bases
  • Drive partner-led opportunities through the full lifecycle, from joint prospecting and deal qualification to close, in close alignment with direct sales teams
  • Develop and execute joint territory and account plans with partners to drive consistent, repeatable enterprise adoption
  • Enable partners through onboarding, training, and ongoing coaching to ensure they can effectively source opportunities, position, sell, and deliver Postman Enterprise
  • Deliver early partner wins while building toward larger, multi-team enterprise deployments and long-term services-led expansion
  • Educate partners and customers on the value of Postman Enterprise throughout the evaluation, adoption, and expansion lifecycle
  • Navigate and influence key decision makers within customer organizations by leveraging partner relationships to expand executive-level awareness and engagement
  • Act as a trusted advisor and strong listener, identify partner and customer needs and collaborate with internal Postman teams to ensure successful outcomes
  • Provide detailed weekly reporting on pipeline health, partner performance, forecasts, and territory progress
  • Communicate, organize, and appropriately escalate partner and customer issues including billing, legal, security, onboarding, and technical inquiries
  • Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineering, Customer Success Managers, Partner Marketing, and Leadership to build and execute strategic adoption and expansion plans in large accounts
  • Provide recommendations based on customer business objectives, usage patterns, and partner capabilities to maximize revenue and long-term value

About You

  • 8+ years of experience in channel sales, partner management, alliances, or partner-focused GTM roles within an enterprise SaaS organization
  • Proven experience working with AWS, systems integrators, resellers, and technology partners to drive partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue
  • Strong understanding of partner-led sales motions, including co-selling, joint account planning, and opportunity orchestration with direct sales teams
  • Experience supporting complex enterprise deals by enabling and influencing partners rather than owning the direct customer close
  • Familiarity with developer platforms, APIs, or highly technical products strongly preferred
  • Ability to build trusted relationships with partner sellers, practice leaders, and executives, as well as internal sales and SE teams
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence without direct authority
  • Comfortable managing multiple partners and opportunities simultaneously across different stages of the sales cycle
  • Data-driven approach to pipeline management, forecasting, and partner performance tracking
  • Track record of driving outcomes through enablement, joint execution, and consistent partner engagement
  • Customer-centric mindset, with a focus on driving successful adoption and long-term value through partners

Postman hires company builders. You enjoy building partner relationships, creating repeatable motions, and helping scale a high-impact channel business as part of one of the best companies in the world.

What Else?

In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.

At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.

Our Values

At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.

Equal opportunity

Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.

description_html:
Who Are We?

Postman is the world’s leading API platform, used by more than 45 million+ developers and 500,000 organizations, including 98% of the Fortune 500. Postman is helping developers and professionals across the globe build the API-first world by simplifying each step of the API lifecycle and streamlining collaboration—enabling users to create better APIs, faster.

The company is headquartered in San Francisco and has offices in Boston, New York, Austin, Tokyo, London, and Bangalore - where Postman was founded. Postman is privately held, with funding from Battery Ventures, BOND, Coatue, CRV, Insight Partners, and Nexus Venture Partners. Learn more at postman.com or connect with Postman on X via @getpostman.

P.S: We highly recommend reading The "API-First World" graphic novel to understand the bigger picture and our vision at Postman.

About The Team

The Channel Sales team at Postman is responsible for scaling our enterprise business through strategic partners, including systems integrators, resellers, and technology alliances. We work cross-functionally with Sales, Solutions Engineering, Marketing, and Customer Success to drive partner-led demand, accelerate enterprise adoption, and expand Postman’s footprint within large organizations.

The Opportunity

As a Channel Sales Manager, you will play a critical role in accelerating Postman Enterprise adoption by developing and executing partner-led sales motions. This role is ideal for a seller who thrives in complex, multi-stakeholder environment

What You’ll Do

  • Own and develop a defined territory by recruiting, enrolling, and activating strategic partners (SIs, resellers, and technology partners) to drive Postman Enterprise sales revenue
  • Build and scale a partner-sourced and partner-influenced pipeline by enabling partners to identify, qualify, and progress opportunities within accounts with large Postman user bases
  • Drive partner-led opportunities through the full lifecycle, from joint prospecting and deal qualification to close, in close alignment with direct sales teams
  • Develop and execute joint territory and account plans with partners to drive consistent, repeatable enterprise adoption
  • Enable partners through onboarding, training, and ongoing coaching to ensure they can effectively source opportunities, position, sell, and deliver Postman Enterprise
  • Deliver early partner wins while building toward larger, multi-team enterprise deployments and long-term services-led expansion
  • Educate partners and customers on the value of Postman Enterprise throughout the evaluation, adoption, and expansion lifecycle
  • Navigate and influence key decision makers within customer organizations by leveraging partner relationships to expand executive-level awareness and engagement
  • Act as a trusted advisor and strong listener, identify partner and customer needs and collaborate with internal Postman teams to ensure successful outcomes
  • Provide detailed weekly reporting on pipeline health, partner performance, forecasts, and territory progress
  • Communicate, organize, and appropriately escalate partner and customer issues including billing, legal, security, onboarding, and technical inquiries
  • Collaborate closely with Solutions Engineering, Customer Success Managers, Partner Marketing, and Leadership to build and execute strategic adoption and expansion plans in large accounts
  • Provide recommendations based on customer business objectives, usage patterns, and partner capabilities to maximize revenue and long-term value

About You

  • 8+ years of experience in channel sales, partner management, alliances, or partner-focused GTM roles within an enterprise SaaS organization
  • Proven experience working with AWS, systems integrators, resellers, and technology partners to drive partner-sourced and partner-influenced revenue
  • Strong understanding of partner-led sales motions, including co-selling, joint account planning, and opportunity orchestration with direct sales teams
  • Experience supporting complex enterprise deals by enabling and influencing partners rather than owning the direct customer close
  • Familiarity with developer platforms, APIs, or highly technical products strongly preferred
  • Ability to build trusted relationships with partner sellers, practice leaders, and executives, as well as internal sales and SE teams
  • Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence without direct authority
  • Comfortable managing multiple partners and opportunities simultaneously across different stages of the sales cycle
  • Data-driven approach to pipeline management, forecasting, and partner performance tracking
  • Track record of driving outcomes through enablement, joint execution, and consistent partner engagement
  • Customer-centric mindset, with a focus on driving successful adoption and long-term value through partners

Postman hires company builders. You enjoy building partner relationships, creating repeatable motions, and helping scale a high-impact channel business as part of one of the best companies in the world.

What Else?

In addition to Postman's pay-on-performance philosophy, and a flexible schedule working with a fun, collaborative team, Postman offers a comprehensive set of benefits, including full medical coverage, flexible PTO, wellness reimbursement, and a monthly lunch stipend. Along with that, our wellness programs will help you stay in the best of your physical and mental health. Our frequent and fascinating team-building events will keep you connected, while our donation-matching program can support the causes you care about. We’re building a long-term company with an inclusive culture where everyone can be the best version of themselves.

At Postman we value in person collaboration. We are in office 5 days a week for all roles based out of our hubs in San Francisco Bay Area, Boston, Austin, Tokyo and London. For roles based in Bangalore, employees currently work in the office three days a week and will transition to five days per week by the end of the year. We were thoughtful in our approach which is based on collaboration and grounded in feedback from our workforce, leadership team, and peers. The benefits of our in office model will be shared knowledge, brainstorming sessions, communication, and building trust in-person that cannot be replicated via zoom.

Our Values

At Postman, we create with the same curiosity that we see in our users. We value transparency and honest communication about not only successes, but also failures. In our work, we focus on specific goals that add up to a larger vision. Our inclusive work culture ensures that everyone is valued equally as important pieces of our final product. We are dedicated to delivering the best products we can.

Equal opportunity

Postman is an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender perception or identity, national origin, age, marital status, protected veteran status, or disability status. Headhunters and recruitment agencies may not submit resumes/CVs through this website or directly to managers. Postman does not accept unsolicited headhunter and agency resumes. Postman will not pay fees to any third-party agency or company that does not have a signed agreement with Postman.

title: Sales and Channel Manager
descriptionhtml:
NFU Energy is looking for a commercially focused Sales & Channel Manager to lead our consultancy sales function and help drive the next phase of growth.

Making sure you fit the guidelines as an applicant for this role is essential, please read the below carefully.

This is a hands-on leadership role with real scope to make an impact. You’ll be responsible for improving team performance, strengthening pipeline visibility and conversion, and developing channel partnerships that generate sustainable new business.

What you’ll do

Lead from the front, coaching and developing the sales team to achieve revenue targets

Improve pipeline management, forecasting, CRM discipline, and overall sales effectiveness

Develop and grow channel, partner, and intermediary relationships to generate opportunities

Support the progression of key strategic opportunities

Work closely with consultancy and marketing teams to align go-to-market activity

Provide clear reporting, insight, and performance visibility

What we’re looking for

Experience leading sales teams in a business-to-business, consultative environment

Demonstrable success in consultative B2B sales, ideally in energy solutions, services, or a related advisory environment

Strong background in channel, partnerships, or indirect sales

Commercially astute, with the ability to improve performance and drive growth

Confident engaging with senior stakeholders

Why join us

You’ll join a business with strong market credibility and a real opportunity to grow its consultancy offer. You’ll have the scope to improve how the team performs, build valuable partnerships, and make a visible commercial impact. xlqdzyr

You may have experience of the following: Sales Manager, Business Development Manager, Commercial Manager, Head of Sales, Sales Leader, Revenue Manager, Growth Manager, Channel Sales Manager, Partner Manager, Alliances Manager, Indirect Sales Manager, Consultancy Sales Manager, Professional Services Sales Lead, Solutions Sales Manager, Strategic Accounts Manager, Enterprise Sales Manager.

REF-(Apply online only)

title: Channel Manager - SaaS - Iberia
descriptionhtml:
• Job Title: Partner Manager – EMEA - Iberia and Italy

• Salary Range: £100,000 - £115,000 (60/40 Split)

• Location / Working Model: London | Hybrid (3 days office / 2 days remote)


Our client is expanding its partner ecosystem across EMEA and is seeking an experienced Partner Manager – EMEA to strengthen relationships with strategic channel partners and drive regional growth. The role has been created as part of continued international expansion, reflecting strong demand for solutions that improve software quality, reliability and security across modern development teams.


We are working with a global technology company that empowers developers and organisations to build secure, maintainable applications by identifying code quality and security issues before they reach production. Their platform supports more than 30 programming languages and is trusted by millions of developers worldwide. With deep roots in the open-source community and a strong mission to help development teams build better software, the organisation offers an innovative, collaborative environment where the Partner Manager – Iberia & Italy can make a meaningful impact while building a thriving partner ecosystem.


The Partner Manager – Iberia & Italy will build, develop and expand relationships with consulting partners, system integrators, value-added resellers and technology alliances across the region.


Role Highlights – The Partner Manager – Iberia & Italy will:


– Identify, evaluate and recruit strategic partners across the Iberia and Italy

– Develop partner territory plans focused on pipeline growth and bookings

– Build executive relationships within partner organisations

– Lead joint business planning and quarterly business reviews

– Collaborate with sales, marketing and technical teams to drive partner success


You Will Need – The role requires:


– 7+ years’ experience in channel or partner management within B2B software

– Professional language in Spanish or Italian

– Strong background in SaaS or subscription-based technology environments

– Proven success building high-performing partner ecosystems across EMEA

– Excellent communication and relationship-building skills at senior levels

– Experience working with key VAR partners such as Softcat, Bytes, Computacenter or Insight


Why You’ll Love It:


– Competitive salary with performance-based bonus potential

– Opportunity to shape and expand a growing partner ecosystem

– Work with widely adopted developer technology used globally

– Flexible hybrid working environment with strong team collaboration

– Culture focused on transparency, learning and continuous improvement


Apply today to grow your career with a forward-thinking employer committed to equal

opportunity. We are committed to promoting equality of opportunity for all employees and job applicants. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we strive to create and maintain a working environment in which everyone is able to make the best use of their skills, free from discrimination or harassment, and in which all decisions are based on merit.

Due to a high number of applicants, we are only able to respond to successful candidates

description_html:
• Job Title: Partner Manager – EMEA - Iberia and Italy

• Salary Range: £100,000 - £115,000 (60/40 Split)

• Location / Working Model: London | Hybrid (3 days office / 2 days remote)


Our client is expanding its partner ecosystem across EMEA and is seeking an experienced Partner Manager – EMEA to strengthen relationships with strategic channel partners and drive regional growth. The role has been created as part of continued international expansion, reflecting strong demand for solutions that improve software quality, reliability and security across modern development teams.


We are working with a global technology company that empowers developers and organisations to build secure, maintainable applications by identifying code quality and security issues before they reach production. Their platform supports more than 30 programming languages and is trusted by millions of developers worldwide. With deep roots in the open-source community and a strong mission to help development teams build better software, the organisation offers an innovative, collaborative environment where the Partner Manager – Iberia & Italy can make a meaningful impact while building a thriving partner ecosystem.


The Partner Manager – Iberia & Italy will build, develop and expand relationships with consulting partners, system integrators, value-added resellers and technology alliances across the region.


Role Highlights – The Partner Manager – Iberia & Italy will:


– Identify, evaluate and recruit strategic partners across the Iberia and Italy

– Develop partner territory plans focused on pipeline growth and bookings

– Build executive relationships within partner organisations

– Lead joint business planning and quarterly business reviews

– Collaborate with sales, marketing and technical teams to drive partner success


You Will Need – The role requires:


– 7+ years’ experience in channel or partner management within B2B software

– Professional language in Spanish or Italian

– Strong background in SaaS or subscription-based technology environments

– Proven success building high-performing partner ecosystems across EMEA

– Excellent communication and relationship-building skills at senior levels

– Experience working with key VAR partners such as Softcat, Bytes, Computacenter or Insight


Why You’ll Love It:


– Competitive salary with performance-based bonus potential

– Opportunity to shape and expand a growing partner ecosystem

– Work with widely adopted developer technology used globally

– Flexible hybrid working environment with strong team collaboration

– Culture focused on transparency, learning and continuous improvement


Apply today to grow your career with a forward-thinking employer committed to equal

opportunity. We are committed to promoting equality of opportunity for all employees and job applicants. In line with the Equality Act 2010, we strive to create and maintain a working environment in which everyone is able to make the best use of their skills, free from discrimination or harassment, and in which all decisions are based on merit.

Due to a high number of applicants, we are only able to respond to successful candidates
reason: Involves developing strategic partnerships with channel providers in a B2B software environment.

title: Senior Channel Manager, eCommerce UK & Ireland
descriptionhtml:
Job Description

We have an opportunity to come join our UK & Ireland (UKI) B2B eCommerce team to drive an outstanding LEGO® digital shopper and consumer experience with our retail partners. Bring expertise in eCommerce, knowledge of best-in-class commercial approaches to help us reach more children and adult fans at one of the most loved brands!



  • No relocation support offered for this role****




Core Responsibilities



  • Strategy Set out a clear vision for the future of the online shopper product journey, and a prioritised roadmap for how to achieve this vision
  • Corporate events Drive commercial performance of the top eCommerce accounts by strategically activating in retailer’s corporate occasions and throughout the year.
  • Drive period calendar (DPC) Be a clear commercial voice and consultative partner collaborating with Trade Marketing teams to ensure the DPC plans are of high quality and aligned to core objectives set out in the joint business plan (JBP).
  • Shopper journey & Digital shelf Optimise the shopper journey to find and enjoy our products on retail partner sites. Partner closely with the Digital Merchandising Coordinator to ensure a strong digital shelf and focus on the key areas of optimisation.
  • Test & Learn Push retailer boundaries by driving innovation, network with internal partners both regionally and globally sharing knowledge, capturing findings and initiatives to copy and scale.
  • Budgets Own the retail media budget across all eCommerce retailers. Consult with Account Managers on conditional terms and allocation towards different initiatives
  • Leadership Function as a mentor and sparring partner for the eCommerce team members, with day-to-day people management of the eCommerce Channel Associate who runs our sponsored search and display retailer marketing



Play your part in our team succeeding!

The team works across our eCommerce and Omni-Channel retailer space delivering best in class Digital Shopper experiences across retail partner digital platforms. The role also collaborates and coaches across the Sales team, the wider Business Unit and across the Western Europe market, to drive Digital and Ecommerce innovation.

Do you have what it takes?



  • Full understanding of the digital retail landscape, including the dynamics of both Pureplay eCommerce and Omnichannel
  • Experience of optimising the retailer digital shopper journey (direct or indirect), owning/ setting the vision, and driving this forward
  • Experience of utilising shopper acquisition channels including search, social and affiliate marketing and understanding key technical issues, and how they could be resolved
  • Experience owning and managing retailer budgets
  • Consistent track record of developing shopper insights via strong analytical approach and an ability to turn analysis into the creation, execution of actionable eCommerce programs and digital campaigns.
  • Strong networking, relationship building, and interpersonal skills.
  • Fluent in English both written and spoken.
  • Able to work 3 days per week in our Slough UK & Ireland HQ



Join the global LEGO® team

What’s in it for you? Here are some of what to expect



  • Family Care Leave – We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.
  • Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.
  • Wellness – We want you to be your best self, so you’ll have access to the Headspace App and lots of wellbeing initiatives and programmes run by local teams where you are based
  • Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.
  • Bonus – We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached, you’ll be rewarded through our global bonus scheme.
  • Your workplace – Our offices will be your primary workplace with flexibility to work from home during your working week. Your People Leader will discuss the principles of our hybrid work model, role eligibility, and frequency with you.



Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic, and inclusive environment of play where everyone feels safe, valued, and they belong.

The LEGO Group is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay regardless of e.g., race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, or gender identity.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Build your career brick by brick at the LEGO Group.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

  • Fluent in English both written and spoken.

title: Channel Manager, EMEA
descriptionhtml:
At Vanta, our mission is to help businesses earn and prove trust. We believe that security should be monitored and verified continuously, and we empower companies to practice better security and prove it with ease. Vanta has a kind and talented team, and while some have prior security experience, many have been successful at Vanta without it.

As a Channel Manager for the EMEA region, you'll manage Vanta channel partnerships across the entire EMEA region, driving our partners to deploy Vanta more broadly. You'll activate sales and services oriented partners to accelerate pipeline and revenue growth while working cross-functionally with our sales teams.

This role sits within Vanta’s Partnerships team, where you will have a direct role in bringing Vanta’s vision of securing every technology company channel partners. You will help us ensure that our partners always receive quality guidance and enablement as they go-to-market with Vanta.

This role will be based from either our Dublin or London office with an office-centric hybrid schedule. The standard in-office days are Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday.

What you’ll do as a Channel Manager for the EMEA region at Vanta:

  • Drive net new pipeline and ARR for the EMEA region via a book of channel partners supporting the downmarket customer segment
  • Co-sell with Account Executives on our Early Stage team to close partner-sourced and partner-influenced deals
  • Enable and build relationships with existing Vanta partners across EMEA
  • Deploy a one-to-many approach to developing business across the region
  • Build one-to-one relationships with key partners
  • Manage your pipeline effectively, working alongside Account Executives to drive deals forward to closed won
  • Identify and acquire high-fit partners to roll into the partner program
  • Maintain strong pipeline rigor and partner activity playbooks
  • Confidently onboard, enable, and help partners close deals

How to be successful in this role:

  • Must be able to work 3 days per week in our Dublin or London offices with 2 days at home
  • 4+ years of channel partner experience working within software/SaaS
  • Must be able to work 3 days per week in our Dublin or London offices with 2 days at home
  • Co-selling experience with demonstrated ability to work alongside direct sales teams
  • Excellent cross-functional collaboration skills with ability to execute and contribute to strategy
  • Proactive self-starter who thrives in a dynamic environment
  • Customer-focused approach with strong communication skills
  • Experience working with resellers, VARs and MSPs
  • Understanding of IT Service Providers and the IT Channel
  • Open to using AI to amplify their skills and strengthen their work - demonstrating curiosity, a willingness to learn, and sound judgment in applying AI responsibly to improve efficiency and impact
  • Open to using AI to amplify their skills and strengthen their work - demonstrating curiosity, a willingness to learn, and sound judgment in applying AI responsibly to improve efficiency and impact.

What you can expect as a Vanta’n:

  • Industry-competitive salary and equity
  • 100% covered medical, dental, and vision benefits with dependents coverage
  • 16 weeks paid Parental Leave for all new parents
  • Health & wellness stipend
  • Remote workspace, internet, and mobile phone stipend
  • Commuter benefits for team members who attend the office
  • Pension matching
  • 25 days of Annual Leave per year and unlimited sick time
  • 8 company-paid holidays
  • Virtual team building activities, lunch and learns, and other company-wide events!
  • Offices in SF, NYC, London, Dublin, Tel Aviv, and Sydney

At Vanta, we are committed to hiring diverse talent of different backgrounds and as such, it is important to us to provide an inclusive work environment for all. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, gender identity, age, religion, sexual orientation, veteran or disability status, or any other protected class. As an equal opportunity employer, we encourage and welcome people of all backgrounds to apply.

About Vanta

We started in 2018, in the wake of several high-profile data breaches. Online security was only becoming more important, but we knew firsthand how hard it could be for fast-growing companies to invest the time and manpower it takes to build a solid security foundation. Vanta was inspired by a vision to restore trust in internet businesses by enabling companies to improve and prove their security. From our early days automating security monitoring for compliance standards like SOC 2, HIPAA and ISO 27001 to creating the world's leading Trust Management Platform, our vision remains unchanged.

Now more than ever, making security continuous—not just a point-in-time check— is essential. Thousands of companies rely on Vanta to build, maintain and demonstrate their trust— all in a way that's real-time and transparent.

Referral Instructions

If you are being referred for the role, please contact that person to apply on your behalf.

title: APAC Channel Manager - Broadcast
descriptionhtml:
Tips: APAC Sales Manager – Broadcast Technology


UK-Based | High-Travel Role (Approx. 50%+ International Travel)


I am currently working with a leading global manufacturer of professional broadcast audio solutions to recruit an International Sales Manager responsible for the APAC region.


This is a senior, high-impact international role requiring extensive travel across Asia-Pacific. It is best suited to a UK-based sales professional who is comfortable operating in a highly mobile position and has a strong understanding of the broadcast or ProAV hardware sales channel.


The Opportunity


You will take full ownership of the APAC region, driving new business while developing strategic accounts and managing established channel partners.

This is a visible, revenue-critical role requiring regular in-market presence, relationship building at senior level, and the ability to navigate complex international sales environments.


Key Responsibilities


  • Deliver and exceed annual sales targets across APAC
  • Drive new business development across key territories
  • Develop and grow strategic accounts and key partners
  • Manage and optimise performance of distributors and channel partners
  • Build strong relationships with system integrators and senior stakeholders
  • Lead complex, high-value, project-led sales cycles
  • Manage tender and bid processes
  • Maintain a consistent in-region presence through frequent travel, trade shows and customer engagement
  • Maintain accurate pipeline management and CRM reporting
  • Act as a key commercial representative for the business across APAC


Candidate Profile

I am looking for a senior international sales professional with a strong track record of selling broadcast or ProAV hardware solutions into APAC.


Essential Experience
  • UK-based with significant experience selling broadcast or ProAV hardware products into APAC markets
  • Strong understanding of the international sales channel, including distributors, resellers and system integrators
  • Proven experience managing and developing channel partners across Asia-Pacific
  • Track record of success in high-value, project-based sales environments
  • Experience working with broadcasters, production companies or AV integrators
  • Strong commercial, negotiation and closing skills
  • Highly organised with strong forecasting and pipeline management capability
  • Comfortable operating in a high-travel role (circa 50%+ international travel)
Desirable
  • Knowledge of broadcast workflows, IP-based systems or AoIP
  • Existing network within APAC broadcast or ProAV markets

    • Additional Information
    • UK-based role
    • High travel requirement (approx. 50%+ international travel)
    • 37.5-hour working week
    • £70-80k basic + commission

    This is an excellent opportunity for a senior sales professional who thrives in an international, travel-heavy role and understands how to drive revenue through established broadcast and ProAV channel ecosystems.

description_html:
Tips: APAC Sales Manager – Broadcast Technology


UK-Based | High-Travel Role (Approx. 50%+ International Travel)


I am currently working with a leading global manufacturer of professional broadcast audio solutions to recruit an International Sales Manager responsible for the APAC region.


This is a senior, high-impact international role requiring extensive travel across Asia-Pacific. It is best suited to a UK-based sales professional who is comfortable operating in a highly mobile position and has a strong understanding of the broadcast or ProAV hardware sales channel.


The Opportunity


You will take full ownership of the APAC region, driving new business while developing strategic accounts and managing established channel partners.

This is a visible, revenue-critical role requiring regular in-market presence, relationship building at senior level, and the ability to navigate complex international sales environments.


Key Responsibilities


  • Deliver and exceed annual sales targets across APAC
  • Drive new business development across key territories
  • Develop and grow strategic accounts and key partners
  • Manage and optimise performance of distributors and channel partners
  • Build strong relationships with system integrators and senior stakeholders
  • Lead complex, high-value, project-led sales cycles
  • Manage tender and bid processes
  • Maintain a consistent in-region presence through frequent travel, trade shows and customer engagement
  • Maintain accurate pipeline management and CRM reporting
  • Act as a key commercial representative for the business across APAC


Candidate Profile

I am looking for a senior international sales professional with a strong track record of selling broadcast or ProAV hardware solutions into APAC.


Essential Experience
  • UK-based with significant experience selling broadcast or ProAV hardware products into APAC markets
  • Strong understanding of the international sales channel, including distributors, resellers and system integrators
  • Proven experience managing and developing channel partners across Asia-Pacific
  • Track record of success in high-value, project-based sales environments
  • Experience working with broadcasters, production companies or AV integrators
  • Strong commercial, negotiation and closing skills
  • Highly organised with strong forecasting and pipeline management capability
  • Comfortable operating in a high-travel role (circa 50%+ international travel)
Desirable
  • Knowledge of broadcast workflows, IP-based systems or AoIP
  • Existing network within APAC broadcast or ProAV markets

    • Additional Information
    • UK-based role
    • High travel requirement (approx. 50%+ international travel)
    • 37.5-hour working week
    • £70-80k basic + commission

    This is an excellent opportunity for a senior sales professional who thrives in an international, travel-heavy role and understands how to drive revenue through established broadcast and ProAV channel ecosystems.
reason: This role involves driving sales through channel partners in the APAC region.

title: Partner Channel Manager
descriptionhtml:
Job Description

WHY THIS ROLE IS A GAME-CHANGER

Are you a dynamic, results-driven sales professional ready to make your mark in the explosive world of AI infrastructure and mission-critical technology? This is your opportunity to drive aggressive revenue growth at the forefront of the digital revolution, partnering with leading IT resellers across the UK & Ireland to power the data centers, AI compute environments, and high-performance computing solutions that are shaping our future.

At Vertiv, we empower the people that will power the future.

THE OPPORTUNITY

As our Partner Manager, you'll be the driving force behind explosive growth in our indirect reseller channel. You'll work with large, diversified IT resellers, covering innovative SMEs to global Blue Chip corporations and government entities, to deliver cutting-edge infrastructure solutions that support the next generation of technology, including AI compute chipsets, high-performance computing, and White Space IT hardware / services offerings.

What You'll Sell (And Why It Matters)

Mission-Critical Hardware: 1-phase and 3-phase UPS systems, advanced rack and row-based cooling (air and liquid), power distribution, and intelligent IT management solutions

Innovative Software: Power and thermal management control, remote access systems, visualization platforms, and protocol interfaces for IT/facilities/DCIM/BMS integration

Value-Added Services: Extended warranty, commissioning, maintenance, and full-spectrum project management for critical infrastructure environments

YOUR IMPACT

Drive Revenue & Build Partnerships

  • Create and execute high-impact Business Growth Plans with managed resellers, setting ambitious targets and smashing sales KPIs
  • Own the relationship from strategy to execution, becoming the trusted advisor your partners can't live without
  • Negotiate and close complex deals through effective deal registration, SPR management, and channel conflict resolution

Enable & Empower Your Partners

  • Elevate partner capabilities through frequent customer visits, technical enablement sessions, and collaboration with Vertiv Application Engineers
  • Lead promotional campaigns, marketing plans, product launches, technology workshops, and seminars that position your partners as market leaders
  • Champion Vertiv's Partner Program, driving adoption of our Partner Portal and cutting-edge configurator tools

Strategic Account Management

  • Conduct site surveys and scope customer requirements with precision
  • Manage the full Quote-to-Order process, leveraging Vertiv's configuration tools and Application Engineering team to deliver winning proposals
  • Master CRM excellence, providing accurate pipeline management and forecasting that drives business intelligence
  • Deliver monthly and quarterly business reviews that showcase your strategic impact

What Makes You The Perfect Fit

Your Track Record Speaks Volumes:

  • 5+ years of exceptional channel development and management experience with a proven track record of over achieving sales targets
  • Deep expertise in the UK & Ireland IT reseller landscape, including IT Distribution, VARs, System Integrators, and white space ecosystem players
  • Bachelor's degree in technology or business with demonstrated business acumen

Your Skills Set You Apart

  • Exceptional relationship builder with outstanding people management capabilities
  • Compelling communicator with excellent presentation skills (in-person and virtual)
  • Strategic thinker who can develop and execute complex business plans with urgency and precision
  • Data-driven decision maker experienced with PowerBI and advanced reporting tools
  • Advanced English proficiency for international collaboration

Bonus Points For

  • Experience in the critical power and cooling infratructure space, including data centres and enterprise verticals
  • Knowledge of server OEM market leaders, their technologies and go-to-market strategies
  • Experience in AI infrastructure, high-performance computing, or data center solutions

YOUR SUCCESS PROFILE

You're a driven self-starter with an unrelenting sense of urgency. You thrive on:

  • Owning your territory and approaching every opportunity with sound ethics, responsibility, and discipline
  • Building lasting relationships while negotiating adeptly and managing conflict with finesse
  • Thinking big and executing with analytical precision and creative problem-solving
  • Continuous improvement, bringing innovative ideas that add value to customers, your team, and the organization
  • Collaboration and knowledge sharing, contributing to team success while driving your own professional growth

WHY VERTIV?

Join a Company Aligned With Strategic Priorities That Matter

  • ✓ Customer Focus - Everything we do starts with the customer
  • ✓ Operational Excellence - Best-in-class processes and execution
  • ✓ High-Performance Culture - Where top performers thrive
  • ✓ Innovation - Leading the industry in next-gen solutions
  • ✓ Financial Strength - Stability and growth opportunity

Our Core Principles: Safety | Integrity | Respect | Teamwork | Diversity & Inclusion

Our Behaviors: Own It | Act With Urgency | Foster a Customer-First Mindset | Think Big and Execute | Lead by Example | Drive Continuous Improvement | Learn and Seek Out Development

READY TO POWER THE FUTURE?

This isn't just a sales role—it's your platform to drive transformation in one of the most exciting technology sectors today. If you're ready to leverage your channel expertise, build powerful partnerships, and achieve exceptional results, we want to hear from you.

title: Senior Operations Manager (Channels)
descriptionhtml:
We're Houst 🏠: A professional property management service. Our mission is to make hosting hassle-free, using technology to disrupt the short-term accommodation sector. We've grown fast. Since launching in 2015, we've partnered with thousands of happy hosts, helping them to maximise their income with minimal stress. The business has now expanded worldwide - we're operating in over 20 cities globally, from Auckland to Lisbon, and there's a lot more to come. From holidaymakers to full-time landlords, host happiness is central to everything we do.

About the Role:

We're looking for a Senior Operations Manager to lead our Channels team and drive strategic projects across the business. This role is crucial for expanding Houst's presence across booking platforms, conducting strategic pricing experiments, and implementing innovative revenue optimisation strategies. You'll manage a team responsible for day-to-day channel operations, tackle complex integration challenges, and collaborate with diverse functions to deliver growth.

What you'll do:

  • Expand Channels: Develop and execute strategies to grow our reach across booking platforms. Oversee seamless onboarding, API integrations, and functionality
  • Optimise Revenue: Experiment with pricing strategies to maximise nightly revenue, balancing occupancy with profitability
  • Track Performance: Analyse metrics to identify underperformance and implement data-driven solutions
  • Streamline Processes: Refine team workflows to enhance productivity and achieve quarterly OKRs
  • Lead the Team: Manage and mentor a high-performing team
  • Drive Strategic Projects: Oversee impactful initiatives that boost efficiency, revenue, and client satisfaction
  • Harness Data: Leverage tools like Looker, Tableau, and SQL to generate actionable insights and strategic recommendations
  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work with Product, Operations, Guest Experience, and Marketing teams to align on and execute channel strategies

Requirements

  • 5+ years in strategy, operations, or a similar role in a high-growth or technology-driven environment
  • Experienced with pricing strategy and revenue optimisation
  • A track record of managing complex projects and collaborating across teams
  • Proven experience managing an operations team
  • Strong analytical skills and proficiency with tools like Looker, Tableau, and SQL
  • A passion for driving growth, improving processes, and delivering results

Benefits

  • Enviable company culture - we've put time into getting our work culture just right. Regular team social events, company-wide recognition of outstanding work
  • Enhanced Parental Leave - Family comes first. We offer great parental leave to spend time with your new child, regardless of your gender
  • Pension - We provide a pension scheme for all permanent employees, in line with government requirements
  • MacBook - For business use
  • 25 days annual leave - plus an extra day off on your birthday (because who wants to work on their birthday

description_html:
We're Houst 🏠: A professional property management service. Our mission is to make hosting hassle-free, using technology to disrupt the short-term accommodation sector. We've grown fast. Since launching in 2015, we've partnered with thousands of happy hosts, helping them to maximise their income with minimal stress. The business has now expanded worldwide - we're operating in over 20 cities globally, from Auckland to Lisbon, and there's a lot more to come. From holidaymakers to full-time landlords, host happiness is central to everything we do.

About the Role:

We're looking for a Senior Operations Manager to lead our Channels team and drive strategic projects across the business. This role is crucial for expanding Houst's presence across booking platforms, conducting strategic pricing experiments, and implementing innovative revenue optimisation strategies. You'll manage a team responsible for day-to-day channel operations, tackle complex integration challenges, and collaborate with diverse functions to deliver growth.

What you'll do:

  • Expand Channels: Develop and execute strategies to grow our reach across booking platforms. Oversee seamless onboarding, API integrations, and functionality
  • Optimise Revenue: Experiment with pricing strategies to maximise nightly revenue, balancing occupancy with profitability
  • Track Performance: Analyse metrics to identify underperformance and implement data-driven solutions
  • Streamline Processes: Refine team workflows to enhance productivity and achieve quarterly OKRs
  • Lead the Team: Manage and mentor a high-performing team
  • Drive Strategic Projects: Oversee impactful initiatives that boost efficiency, revenue, and client satisfaction
  • Harness Data: Leverage tools like Looker, Tableau, and SQL to generate actionable insights and strategic recommendations
  • Collaborate Across Teams: Work with Product, Operations, Guest Experience, and Marketing teams to align on and execute channel strategies

Requirements

  • 5+ years in strategy, operations, or a similar role in a high-growth or technology-driven environment
  • Experienced with pricing strategy and revenue optimisation
  • A track record of managing complex projects and collaborating across teams
  • Proven experience managing an operations team
  • Strong analytical skills and proficiency with tools like Looker, Tableau, and SQL
  • A passion for driving growth, improving processes, and delivering results

Benefits

  • Enviable company culture - we've put time into getting our work culture just right. Regular team social events, company-wide recognition of outstanding work
  • Enhanced Parental Leave - Family comes first. We offer great parental leave to spend time with your new child, regardless of your gender
  • Pension - We provide a pension scheme for all permanent employees, in line with government requirements
  • MacBook - For business use
  • 25 days annual leave - plus an extra day off on your birthday (because who wants to work on their birthday
