description_html:
About lemon.markets 🍋


We enable anyone to be an investor. Our mission is to grow investing opportunities for all European customers and businesses. By empowering FinTechs, banks, and wealth managers to offer investment products, we help create financial well-being for their customers.


lemon.markets is a Brokerage-as-a-Service platform: simple digital access to capital markets through a single API. Our API-first infrastructure abstracts brokerage complexity with a well-documented API, a self-service customer operations portal, and a clear regulatory framework, so partners can launch customer-centric, compliant products in weeks, not months.


As part of the dwpbank Group, we share a clear goal: we enable anyone to be an investor by making investing in capital markets more accessible. By combining dwpbank’s long-standing expertise and stability with lemon.markets’ technology-first mindset and speed, we are building a modern, modular securities services ecosystem. Together, we offer faster time-to-market, modular services, and a modern customer experience that helps financial service providers stay competitive and respond more flexibly in a changing market.


Your Mission:


At lemon.markets, you’ll own and grow partner/customer relationships end-to-end, from onboarding to launch, day-to-day operations, and expansion. As our Senior Partner Manager, you’re the partner’s main point of contact and their strongest internal advocate, orchestrating cross-functional teams to help partners build successful investment products on top of lemon.markets.

Our partner universe is currently evolving: alongside our direct business, where we typically work with fintechs and neobanks, we were recently acquired by dwpbank and are now building a combined offering with them. This means we’ll be welcoming a new wave of partners, with a strong focus on German banking partners, who will onboard through the joint motion and be supported by Partner Management from day one.

Your role is to make this journey feel customer-centric, structured, and straightforward, while ensuring we meet the regulatory and operational standards required to scale safely and sustainably.


You will:


  • Own customer onboarding: Take partners from handover/intake to go-live by coordinating internal workstreams and driving timelines, decisions, and execution.
  • Lead day-to-day partner management: Act as the single point of contact post-launch, run partner cadences, manage escalations, and represent partner needs internally.
  • Ensure regulatory & outsourcing readiness: Drive third-party risk, compliance, and outsourcing requirements end-to-end and help define/manage SLAs where relevant.
  • Drive partner growth: Identify expansion opportunities, translate them into concrete initiatives, and validate business cases to grow partner value and revenue.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally to improve product-market fit: Partner closely with Go-to-Market, Product, Brokerage, and Operations to feed insights back into our product and services.
  • Build and scale the Partner Management function: Develop frameworks, playbooks, and standards, and document learnings to continuously improve how we work.


You will be successful in this role if:


  • Bring extensive experience in B2B Partner Management, Onboarding, or Project/Program Management, and you’re used to taking clear ownership of cross-functional delivery, driving outcomes beyond your own team.
  • Have experience in the financial industry, ideally in a brokerage, brokerage-related environment, fintech, or banking context and you can quickly build a strong understanding of our business.
  • Have experience managing a diverse partner portfolio across fast-moving fintechs/neobanks and (larger) German banks/corporate banks, adjusting your style to different decision-making cultures and stakeholder landscapes.
  • Have hands-on exposure to regulatory environments and have worked closely with Risk, Compliance, and Outsourcing / vendor management functions to keep delivery smooth while staying within the guardrails.
  • Understand end-to-end partner management and enjoy owning the full lifecycle: onboarding → launch → day to day management → growth/expansion.
  • Communicate exceptionally well in English and German (C1+) building trust quickly, leading tough conversations calmly, and keeping momentum across senior internal and external stakeholders.


How we take care of you:


  • 🚀 Compensation package: We offer you a competitive salary, based on your experience and the value-add you bring to lemon.markets.
  • 🕑 Flexible working hours: We trust people to choose when they work best and avoid a rigid 9 to 5 mindset. You decide on the rhythm that helps you do your best work, with only shared core hours from 12:00 to 16:00 to support collaboration.
  • 🏢 Hybrid work setup: You can work from anywhere in Germany within a hybrid model. At the same time, in-person collaboration is a key part of our culture. That being said, individual contributer lemoneers work 4 days or more per month on-site in our Berlin office, spending quality time together with the team at events, onsites/offsites or over team dinners.
  • 📈 Grow with us: We take your personal development seriously! You’ll get regular mentorship and stretch opportunities, a clear career development framework with frequent feedback conversations as well as an annual learning budget.
  • 🌱 People & culture: You’ll feel our culture in the small things, like polaroids from our lemoneers’ first day, Friday food market strolls at Maybachufer, and our monthly new-joiner breakfast. But also in the bigger things: celebrating the onboarding of new partners, growing our teams, and owning a strong discussion culture.
  • 💛 Perks & benefits: We choose benefits that add actual value. We sponsor a Deutschlandticket for employees on unlimited contracts, plus 50% off on Beatvest - our partner for financial education and investing.
  • 🏝 Workation: Get out of here! At lemon.markets you can work remotely from anywhere in the EU for up to 10 consecutive days at a time, and up to 60 days per year in total.


As an equal opportunity employer, lemon.markets is committed to building an inclusive workplace. The company welcomes people from all different backgrounds, including age, citizenship, ethnic and racial origins, gender identities, individuals with disabilities, marital status, religions and ideologies, and sexual orientations to apply.


If you feel this role could be a great match for you even if you don’t tick every box, we’d still love to hear from you, please apply.


You have any questions? Feel free to contact us via talent@lemon.markets.com
reason: Focuses on managing B2B partner relationships in the financial services sector.

title: Partner Success Manager B2B (m/f/d)
descriptionhtml:
Why we are looking for you

As our Partner Success Manager B2B (m/f/d), you will be responsible for providing world-class support to our campsite partners across Europe and play a key role in the seamless integration of new B2B clients into our platform and internal systems. Through your operational excellence, you will significantly contribute to optimizing our conversion rates and ensuring a premier partner experience.

What You Can Expect

  • First Point of Contact: You will act as the primary ambassador for our campsite partners across Europe, managing their inquiries end-to-end across email, phone, and digital channels with a solution-oriented mindset.
  • Seamless Onboarding: In close collaboration with internal teams such as Product Data, Connectivity, and Finance, you will create and manage new campsite listings. You’ll ensure a smooth onboarding journey and drive fast, reliable activation processes within our backend.
  • Partner Support: As a dedicated guide for our new partners, you will actively support them during the onboarding phase, ensuring a seamless transition into live operations.
  • Data & Content Excellence: You will maintain high-quality campsite data and content in close cooperation with the Product Data team, ensuring our platform remains accurate and appealing.
  • Optimization of Bookability: You will monitor and continuously improve booking availability across all listings to maximize both conversion rates and partner satisfaction.
  • Proactive Problem Solving: You will identify and resolve technical or operational issues affecting bookability, coordinating with internal teams to ensure stable and high-performing booking operations.
  • Voice of the Partner: As a vital link to our internal stakeholders, you will represent the voice of our campsite partners. By documenting feedback and analyzing recurring challenges, you will directly contribute to the continuous improvement of our internal processes.

What You Should Bring

  • You bring a proven track record in B2B support, partner success, or a similar operational field. You are familiar with call center structures or dynamic, high-volume support environments.
  • Prior experience with connectivity systems (e.g., PMS or Channel Managers) and CRM software (ideally Salesforce) is a distinct advantage.
  • You possess excellent coordination skills and the ability to communicate confidently and effectively across various internal teams.
  • A proactive, problem-solving mindset is second nature to you, always keeping the specific needs and success of our partners at the forefront.
  • You stand out for your high level of empathy and a deep-seated commitment to service excellence.
  • A full professional proficiency in German and English (C1/B2) is mandatory for this role, as you will be handling complex inquiries in both languages on a daily basis. Proficiency in Dutch and/or French is a definite plus, though not a requirement.

What We Offer

  • Flexible working hours, home office, working from abroad, hybrid working
  • 30 vacation days
  • A camper for your short trip or vacation
  • Best hardware of your choice, modern tools
  • Health promotion, e.g. through subsidies for Urban Sports Club or ergonomic workstations, free fruit, vegetables and muesli
  • Regular company parties and events
  • Various employee discounts, e.g. Travel Industry Card
  • An appreciative work culture and atmosphere in an international team
  • A good mix of agile start-up and grown professional structures
  • Regular feedback as well as internal and external training, e.g. at our PiNCAMP Academy
  • A steep learning curve with a lot of responsibility from day one, flat hierarchies, quick decisions, room for creative ideas and new impulses
  • Modern office in the heart of Berlin with excellent public transport connections and a rooftop terrace for fresh air and sun-bathing

About Us

PiNCAMP is the camping startup of ADAC, ANWB and TCS - in Germany, the Netherlands and Switzerland, as well as throughout Europe. An international team of more than 80 digital experts digitises the camping industry by combining the experience and reputation of our shareholders with the speed of an agile start-up. Our goal is to be Europe's leading camping platform. For our further growth we are looking for passionate hands-on people who want to grow with us and are eager to build an excellent product for campers and campsites alike.

description_html:
Why we are looking for you

As our Partner Success Manager B2B (m/f/d), you will be responsible for providing world-class support to our campsite partners across Europe and play a key role in the seamless integration of new B2B clients into our platform and internal systems. Through your operational excellence, you will significantly contribute to optimizing our conversion rates and ensuring a premier partner experience.

What You Can Expect

  • First Point of Contact: You will act as the primary ambassador for our campsite partners across Europe, managing their inquiries end-to-end across email, phone, and digital channels with a solution-oriented mindset.
  • Seamless Onboarding: In close collaboration with internal teams such as Product Data, Connectivity, and Finance, you will create and manage new campsite listings. You’ll ensure a smooth onboarding journey and drive fast, reliable activation processes within our backend.
  • Partner Support: As a dedicated guide for our new partners, you will actively support them during the onboarding phase, ensuring a seamless transition into live operations.
  • Data & Content Excellence: You will maintain high-quality campsite data and content in close cooperation with the Product Data team, ensuring our platform remains accurate and appealing.
  • Optimization of Bookability: You will monitor and continuously improve booking availability across all listings to maximize both conversion rates and partner satisfaction.
  • Proactive Problem Solving: You will identify and resolve technical or operational issues affecting bookability, coordinating with internal teams to ensure stable and high-performing booking operations.
  • Voice of the Partner: As a vital link to our internal stakeholders, you will represent the voice of our campsite partners. By documenting feedback and analyzing recurring challenges, you will directly contribute to the continuous improvement of our internal processes.

What You Should Bring

  • You bring a proven track record in B2B support, partner success, or a similar operational field. You are familiar with call center structures or dynamic, high-volume support environments.
  • Prior experience with connectivity systems (e.g., PMS or Channel Managers) and CRM software (ideally Salesforce) is a distinct advantage.
  • You possess excellent coordination skills and the ability to communicate confidently and effectively across various internal teams.
  • A proactive, problem-solving mindset is second nature to you, always keeping the specific needs and success of our partners at the forefront.
  • You stand out for your high level of empathy and a deep-seated commitment to service excellence.
  • A full professional proficiency in German and English (C1/B2) is mandatory for this role, as you will be handling complex inquiries in both languages on a daily basis. Proficiency in Dutch and/or French is a definite plus, though not a requirement.

What We Offer

  • Flexible working hours, home office, working from abroad, hybrid working
  • 30 vacation days
  • A camper for your short trip or vacation
  • Best hardware of your choice, modern tools
  • Health promotion, e.g. through subsidies for Urban Sports Club or ergonomic workstations, free fruit, vegetables and muesli
  • Regular company parties and events
  • Various employee discounts, e.g. Travel Industry Card
  • An appreciative work culture and atmosphere in an international team
  • A good mix of agile start-up and grown professional structures
  • Regular feedback as well as internal and external training, e.g. at our PiNCAMP Academy
  • A steep learning curve with a lot of responsibility from day one, flat hierarchies, quick decisions, room for creative ideas and new impulses
  • Modern office in the heart of Berlin with excellent public transport connections and a rooftop terrace for fresh air and sun-bathing

About Us

PiNCAMP is the camping startup of ADAC, ANWB and TCS - in Germany, the Netherlands and Switzerland, as well as throughout Europe. An international team of more than 80 digital experts digitises the camping industry by combining the experience and reputation of our shareholders with the speed of an agile start-up. Our goal is to be Europe's leading camping platform. For our further growth we are looking for passionate hands-on people who want to grow with us and are eager to build an excellent product for campers and campsites alike.

title: Senior Strategic Partnerships Manager DE/PL (f/m/d)
companyindustry: Construction, Software Development, and IT Services and IT Consulting
descriptionhtml:
Total Compensation Range: €64,300 - €101,700

The Position

Germany and Poland sit at the core of Hive's business. Germany is our home market, and Poland is one of our most strategically important fulfillment regions. This role owns end-to-end partner operations for both.

As Senior Strategic Partner Manager DE/PL, you will own Hive's delivery, freight, and fulfillment partner portfolio across Germany and Poland, while leading a small, high-impact team. You'll be accountable for everything from commercial partner relationships down to operational performance at our partner sites, and be the face of operations for these two markets, internally with our Growth Team and externally with our most important carrier and 3PL partners.

You'll do this with two direct reports: a Junior Strategic Partner Manager, and a Special Operations Manager based in Poland.

Your Key Responsibilities:

  • Own the full DE/PL partner portfolio end-to-end (delivery, freight, and fulfilment), from contract terms and commercial performance (DE/PL P&L) through to operational performance (fulfilment health score, SLA adherence, pickup planning), including business reviews with key partners
  • Lead negotiations with Hive's most strategic carrier and fulfilment partners, driving cost efficiency without compromising service
  • Set the strategic vision for the DE/PL network (partner mix, capability gaps)
  • Lead and develop a team of two: set clear targets, coach on both commercial and operational topics, remove blockers, and hold a high barDrive operational performance with your Special Operations Manager, and commercial performance with your Junior
  • Strategic Partner Manager (alongside your own negotiation work)
  • Be the face of Hive Ops for DE/PL, internally with the Growth Team and Merchant Care; externally with carriers, 3PLs, and fulfilment partners
  • Be the go-to escalation point internally for all DE/PL Ops topics across Slack, business reviews, and cross-functional projects

Your Profile

We know, sometimes you can't tick every box. We would still love to hear from you if you think you're a good fit!

  • You have 5+ years of experience in operations, logistics, supply chain, consulting, or a fast-growth startup, ideally with exposure to both commercial partner management and operational/network performance
  • You've led people before, through direct reports, mentoring, or strong matrix leadership, and you know how to set direction, coach, and hold others to a high bar without micromanaging
  • You have a track record of managing commercial relationships end-to-end with carriers, 3PLs, freight or fulfilment providers, or similar external partners, including negotiating contracts and driving cost down
  • You're comfortable getting into operational detail (site performance, SLAs, root-cause analysis) and you actually like spending time on the warehouse floor or in the sorting center, not just in spreadsheets and meeting rooms
  • You're an owner; you take decisions independently, move fast, and treat the DE/PL business like it's your own
  • You are analytically strong and confident using data to drive negotiations, partner conversations, and operational decisions
  • You have excellent communication skills and fluency in English & German

Our offering

  • Build something that matters: Hive is redefining how commerce operations work in a €200B+ market. We already reach tens of millions of consumers every year and empower over 500 independent brands to grow and thrive - and we’re just getting started.
  • Work with exceptional people: Join a high-performing, ambitious team, including many from world-class companies such as McKinsey, Amazon, Shopify, Google, TikTok, and Trade Republic.
  • Grow with Hive: From day one, you’ll take ownership of ambitious projects and grow alongside Hive as we scale. We believe in meritocracy - your impact drives your progression, not your past titles.
  • Competitive compensation: We offer market-competitive salaries and an equity incentive plan (EIP) for all full-time team members.
  • Tools to thrive: Choose the setup that helps you do your best work - a MacBook (or laptop of your choice), plus accessories like an extra monitor or headphones to match your working style.
  • Well-being matters: Enjoy 30 vacation days per year, plus a paid 20-day sabbatical every three years to recharge and reflect.
  • Flexibility and connection: Benefit from flexible working hours, free drinks and snacks at our offices, and regular team events - including company-wide offsites that bring all of Hive together.

About Us

We're revolutionizing e-commerce operations.

At Hive, we empower brands to excel in the digital commerce era through our innovative operations platform. By combining cutting-edge technology with a curated network of top-tier operations partners, we deliver measurable results.

Our comprehensive platform streamlines the entire operational chain through a single, intuitive interface. Since our founding in 2020, we've rapidly grown to become one of Europe's leading operations platform, partnering with hundreds of innovative brands. With strategic locations in Berlin, Paris, Milan, Madrid, London, and Amsterdam. Backed by prestigious investors including Tiger Global, Earlybird, and Picus Capital, we're scaling our impact across Europe.

Diversity and inclusion are core to our success. We actively cultivate an environment where every team member, regardless of background, can thrive. We welcome talent from all walks of life, regardless of religion, ethnicity, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability. At Hive, authenticity and professional growth go hand in hand.

title: Partnerships Manager:in
descriptionhtml:
Coup verschafft innovativen deutschen Unternehmen einfachen Zugang zu staatlichen Fördermitteln. Dafür entwickeln wir eine leistungsstarke Softwareplattform und kombinieren sie mit exzellenter persönlicher Beratung. So wird die Beantragung und Abrechnung von R&D Förderungen für unsere Kund:innen einfach und bequem.


Bei Coup sind wir der Go-to-Partner für die Beantragung und Abwicklung der Forschungszulage (FZuL) in Deutschland und haben damit seit 2021 mehr als 250 Unternehmen über 100 Mio. Euro Fördermittel gesichert.


Join us and help more companies get Couped!


Für die nächste Wachstumsphase legen wir einen starken Fokus auf den Ausbau unseres Partnernetzwerks. Wir suchen eine vertriebsstarke, unternehmerische Person, die Partnerschaften nicht nur verwaltet, sondern aktiv akquiriert und ausbaut.


Aufgaben

Als Partnerships Manager:in bist du das Gesicht von Coup gegenüber unseren wichtigsten Multiplikatoren. Du verantwortest den gesamten Zyklus von der Identifikation über die Akquise bis hin zur Aktivierung von Partnern. Du bist die Schnittstelle zwischen Marketing, Sales und R&D Consulting und übernimmst den Lead für strategisch relevante Projekte im Bereich Partnership Management und Community Building mit einem klaren Ziel:


Durch den systematischen Aufbau starker Netzwerke und Multiplikatoren eine skalierbare Lead-Pipeline zu etablieren, die Coup als vertrauenswürdigen Go-to-Partner für die Forschungszulage stärkt und maßgeblich zu unseren Umsatzzielen beiträgt.


Deine Mission:



  • Partnership Management & Sales: Du bist verantwortlich für den Aufbau und die Skalierung unseres Partner-Netzwerks. Du identifizierst neue Potenziale, betreibst aktiv Akquise von Partner-Accounts und verhandelst Kooperationsmodelle, die eine Win-Win-Situation schaffen.

  • Community Building: Du schaffst Räume für Austausch und Vertrauen. Durch die Konzeption und Steuerung von exklusiven Formaten (Events, Webinare, Roundtables) baust du eine loyale Community auf, die Coup als führende Expertin im Fördermittelmarkt stärkt.

  • GTM-Strategy: Du verantwortest den Erfolg unserer Markteintritte in neue Segmente. Du entwickelst überzeugende Storylines und Sales-Materialien, die komplexe Themen wie Fördermittel für Partner verständlich und attraktiv machen. Dabei verstehst du es, unser Markennarrativ konsequent und nachhaltig weiterzuentwickeln.

  • Sales Enablement & Messen: Du erstellst überzeugende Pitch-Decks und Materialien, mit denen unsere Partner Coup erfolgreich weiterempfehlen können. Zudem repräsentierst du Coup auf Veranstaltungen, Messen und bei Partner-Events.

  • Data & Performance: Du definierst und trackst KPIs für deine Partnerschaften. Analytics und Reportings sind für dich die Basis, um zu entscheiden, in welche Kooperationen wir weiter investieren.


Qualifikation

Du bringst nicht nur Erfahrung, sondern auch den Antrieb mit, Partnerschaften strukturiert aufzubauen und deren Erfolg messbar zu machen. Du verstehst dich als unternehmerisch denkendes Bindeglied zwischen Strategie und Sales:



  • Strategischer Drive: Du verbindest strategische Zielorientierung mit unternehmerischem Handeln und übernimmst Ownership für die erfolgreiche Umsetzung deiner Projekte.

  • Projektmanagement: Du hast Erfahrung in der Steuerung cross-funktionaler Teams und führst Projekte (z. B. Produkt-Launches oder Events) strukturiert von Planung bis Rollout.

  • Analytische Kompetenz: Du denkst in Sales-Funneln. Du bist zahlengetrieben und trackst den Erfolg deiner Partnerschaften anhand von Leads, Conversion Rates und Pipeline-Impact.

  • Konzeptionelle Stärke & Kreativität: Du hast ein exzellentes Gespür für Sprache und Design. Du kannst komplexe Sachverhalte in packende Narrative übersetzen, die unsere Positionierung im Markt widerspiegelt.

  • Kommunikation: Du kommunizierst klar und präzise auf Deutsch und Englisch und bist versiert darin, mit internen und externen Stakeholder:innen (R&D, Sales, Product, Content, VCs, Eventpartner:innen, etc.) zusammenzuarbeiten

  • Hands-on-Mentalität: Dir gefällt es, mehrere Themen gleichzeitig voranzutreiben, und du bist bereit, sowohl taktisch zu planen als auch operativ anzupacken.

  • Erfahrung: Du verfügst über einschlägige Berufserfahrung im Business Development, Strategic Partnerships oder B2B-Sales.

  • Tools: Du hast Erfahrung im Umgang mit Google Suite (insbesondere: Slides), Pipedrive und Projektmanagement-Tools (z.B. notion, Asana, Trello)

  • Standort: Du hast deinen regelmäßigen Aufenthaltsort in Berlin, sodass wir 2–3 Tage pro Woche gemeinsam im Büro arbeiten können.


Benefits

Bei Coup legen wir Wert darauf, dass wir zusammen ein erfolgreiches Unternehmen aufbauen und uns dabei stets außergewöhnlich gut behandeln.


Folgende Werte solltest du daher teilen:


🏆 Kundenfokus: Der Erfolg unserer Kund:innen ist unser Erfolg


🏭 Proaktivität: Wir ergreifen Initiative und übernehmen Ownership


🪢 Aufgeschlossenheit: Wir begrüßen andere Perspektiven und profitieren von ihnen


🖖 Neugier: Wir wollen ständig lernen und feiern Fortschritt


🍭 Spaß: Wir nehmen unsere Arbeit ernst, uns selbst nicht so sehr


Was dich bei Coup erwartet:


🧑‍🎨 Ein dynamisches Umfeld und die Möglichkeit, die Entwicklung von Coup aktiv mitzugestalten


🤲 Viel Verantwortung und Ownership und die Möglichkeit in einem kleinen Team schnell Wirkung zu erzielen


💡 Raum für Kreativität und Eigeninitiative: Deine Ideen finden Gehör und können direkt umgesetzt werden


📈 Persönliche und fachliche Weiterentwicklung: Enge Zusammenarbeit mit unserem Leadership-Team, insbesondere Theresa (CRO), Arne (Gründer + CEO) und Erik (Gründer + CTO)


💻 Modernes technisches Equipment


⏰ Flexible Arbeitszeiten, remote oder im Office in Berlin


💳 Zuschuss zum Jobticket


🌴 30 Tage bezahlter Urlaub


Do you want to help more companies get Couped?


Dann schick uns deinen CV unter Angabe deiner Gehaltsvorstellungen.


Wir freuen uns von dir zu hören!

Coup verschafft innovativen deutschen Unternehmen einfachen Zugang zu staatlichen Fördermitteln. Dafür entwickeln wir eine leistungsstarke Softwareplattform und kombinieren sie mit exzellenter persönlicher Beratung. So wird die Beantragung und Abrechnung von R&D Förderungen für unsere Kund:innen einfach und bequem.


Bei Coup sind wir der Go-to-Partner für die Beantragung und Abwicklung der Forschungszulage (FZuL) in Deutschland und haben damit seit 2021 mehr als 250 Unternehmen über 100 Mio. Euro Fördermittel gesichert.


Join us and help more companies get Couped!


Für die nächste Wachstumsphase legen wir einen starken Fokus auf den Ausbau unseres Partnernetzwerks. Wir suchen eine vertriebsstarke, unternehmerische Person, die Partnerschaften nicht nur verwaltet, sondern aktiv akquiriert und ausbaut.


Aufgaben

Als Partnerships Manager:in bist du das Gesicht von Coup gegenüber unseren wichtigsten Multiplikatoren. Du verantwortest den gesamten Zyklus von der Identifikation über die Akquise bis hin zur Aktivierung von Partnern. Du bist die Schnittstelle zwischen Marketing, Sales und R&D Consulting und übernimmst den Lead für strategisch relevante Projekte im Bereich Partnership Management und Community Building mit einem klaren Ziel:


Durch den systematischen Aufbau starker Netzwerke und Multiplikatoren eine skalierbare Lead-Pipeline zu etablieren, die Coup als vertrauenswürdigen Go-to-Partner für die Forschungszulage stärkt und maßgeblich zu unseren Umsatzzielen beiträgt.


Deine Mission:



  • Partnership Management & Sales: Du bist verantwortlich für den Aufbau und die Skalierung unseres Partner-Netzwerks. Du identifizierst neue Potenziale, betreibst aktiv Akquise von Partner-Accounts und verhandelst Kooperationsmodelle, die eine Win-Win-Situation schaffen.

  • Community Building: Du schaffst Räume für Austausch und Vertrauen. Durch die Konzeption und Steuerung von exklusiven Formaten (Events, Webinare, Roundtables) baust du eine loyale Community auf, die Coup als führende Expertin im Fördermittelmarkt stärkt.

  • GTM-Strategy: Du verantwortest den Erfolg unserer Markteintritte in neue Segmente. Du entwickelst überzeugende Storylines und Sales-Materialien, die komplexe Themen wie Fördermittel für Partner verständlich und attraktiv machen. Dabei verstehst du es, unser Markennarrativ konsequent und nachhaltig weiterzuentwickeln.

  • Sales Enablement & Messen: Du erstellst überzeugende Pitch-Decks und Materialien, mit denen unsere Partner Coup erfolgreich weiterempfehlen können. Zudem repräsentierst du Coup auf Veranstaltungen, Messen und bei Partner-Events.

  • Data & Performance: Du definierst und trackst KPIs für deine Partnerschaften. Analytics und Reportings sind für dich die Basis, um zu entscheiden, in welche Kooperationen wir weiter investieren.


Qualifikation

Du bringst nicht nur Erfahrung, sondern auch den Antrieb mit, Partnerschaften strukturiert aufzubauen und deren Erfolg messbar zu machen. Du verstehst dich als unternehmerisch denkendes Bindeglied zwischen Strategie und Sales:



  • Strategischer Drive: Du verbindest strategische Zielorientierung mit unternehmerischem Handeln und übernimmst Ownership für die erfolgreiche Umsetzung deiner Projekte.

  • Projektmanagement: Du hast Erfahrung in der Steuerung cross-funktionaler Teams und führst Projekte (z. B. Produkt-Launches oder Events) strukturiert von Planung bis Rollout.

  • Analytische Kompetenz: Du denkst in Sales-Funneln. Du bist zahlengetrieben und trackst den Erfolg deiner Partnerschaften anhand von Leads, Conversion Rates und Pipeline-Impact.

  • Konzeptionelle Stärke & Kreativität: Du hast ein exzellentes Gespür für Sprache und Design. Du kannst komplexe Sachverhalte in packende Narrative übersetzen, die unsere Positionierung im Markt widerspiegelt.

  • Kommunikation: Du kommunizierst klar und präzise auf Deutsch und Englisch und bist versiert darin, mit internen und externen Stakeholder:innen (R&D, Sales, Product, Content, VCs, Eventpartner:innen, etc.) zusammenzuarbeiten

  • Hands-on-Mentalität: Dir gefällt es, mehrere Themen gleichzeitig voranzutreiben, und du bist bereit, sowohl taktisch zu planen als auch operativ anzupacken.

  • Erfahrung: Du verfügst über einschlägige Berufserfahrung im Business Development, Strategic Partnerships oder B2B-Sales.

  • Tools: Du hast Erfahrung im Umgang mit Google Suite (insbesondere: Slides), Pipedrive und Projektmanagement-Tools (z.B. notion, Asana, Trello)

  • Standort: Du hast deinen regelmäßigen Aufenthaltsort in Berlin, sodass wir 2–3 Tage pro Woche gemeinsam im Büro arbeiten können.


Benefits

Bei Coup legen wir Wert darauf, dass wir zusammen ein erfolgreiches Unternehmen aufbauen und uns dabei stets außergewöhnlich gut behandeln.


Folgende Werte solltest du daher teilen:


🏆 Kundenfokus: Der Erfolg unserer Kund:innen ist unser Erfolg


🏭 Proaktivität: Wir ergreifen Initiative und übernehmen Ownership


🪢 Aufgeschlossenheit: Wir begrüßen andere Perspektiven und profitieren von ihnen


🖖 Neugier: Wir wollen ständig lernen und feiern Fortschritt


🍭 Spaß: Wir nehmen unsere Arbeit ernst, uns selbst nicht so sehr


Was dich bei Coup erwartet:


🧑‍🎨 Ein dynamisches Umfeld und die Möglichkeit, die Entwicklung von Coup aktiv mitzugestalten


🤲 Viel Verantwortung und Ownership und die Möglichkeit in einem kleinen Team schnell Wirkung zu erzielen


💡 Raum für Kreativität und Eigeninitiative: Deine Ideen finden Gehör und können direkt umgesetzt werden


📈 Persönliche und fachliche Weiterentwicklung: Enge Zusammenarbeit mit unserem Leadership-Team, insbesondere Theresa (CRO), Arne (Gründer + CEO) und Erik (Gründer + CTO)


💻 Modernes technisches Equipment


⏰ Flexible Arbeitszeiten, remote oder im Office in Berlin


💳 Zuschuss zum Jobticket


🌴 30 Tage bezahlter Urlaub


Do you want to help more companies get Couped?


Dann schick uns deinen CV unter Angabe deiner Gehaltsvorstellungen.


Wir freuen uns von dir zu hören!

title: Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager
descriptionhtml:
We are currently recruiting for an Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager for possibly one of the most exciting businesses we have partnered with in the past 12 months.


This is a newly created role for an organisation that is scaling at a phenomenal pace, leading their DACH (Germany, Austria & Switzerland) territory, headquartered in Berlin.


With an unparalleled culture favouring independence and speed over perfection, the successful Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager will work with best-in-class leadership and unlock unlimited opportunities for personal, career, and financial growth.


The organisation’s biggest challenge is acquiring a reliable supply of inventory stock, and the Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager will focus on this challenge with incredibly clear goals.


The successful candidate will take ownership of the organisation’s most strategic partnerships across leasing providers, OEMs, and major retail groups, which will drive the largest share of the organisation’s supply.


Working on the clear goal of securing high-quality and predictable inflows of inventory through agreements with major industry players, the Enterprise Partnerships and Operations Manager will spend 35% of their time developing existing relationships with the remainder on building new relationships


You will work with sophisticated partners who expect senior-level engagement, clear business cases, and fast, professional processes.


The day-to-day duties will include:

  • Strengthening and expanding the business’s strategic B2B partnerships across DACH
  • Managing a large portfolio of key retail chains and OEMs, taking ownership of and growing strategic partnerships, building trust-based relationships, and becoming the key contact for all commercial topics
  • Collaborating closely with operations, product, pricing, logistics, and leadership teams to translate partnerships into scalable inventory growth for the business
  • Leading complex commercial negotiations to form framework agreements, secure favourable purchasing conditions, and ensure alignment across volumes, pricing, and service levels
  • Taking ownership of senior stakeholder relationships to drive recurring volumes, expand to additional locations, and ensure partners view you and the business as their primary resale channel
  • Immersing in companywide and warehouse operations, troubleshooting and building enhanced processes as the operation continues to expand
  • Using data to lead decisions, including working with forecasts, ROI cases, and KPIs to prioritise and manage performance
  • Identifying new commercial levers such as joint campaigns and affiliate models to unlock additional stock volume
  • Enabling a strong partner experience with smooth onboarding, reliable collection of inventory, and an exceptional level of customer service


What we are looking for:

  • 5+ years of experience within business development, enterprise sales, key account management, or strategic buying
  • Robust stakeholder management skills, with a proven track record of delivering results across complex and long sales cycles and building high-level credibility with senior stakeholders
  • An excellent track record of securing structured agreements which balance partner value with the business’s economics
  • Fluent English and German written and verbal communication skills


Why you should consider this business:

  • Join a rapidly scaling business that has already secured Series C funding, backed by top-tier investors during a decisive expansion phase
  • Earn a competitive basic salary in addition to a joining bonus and an annual bonus offered in the form of stock options. The business is set to explode over the coming 5 years, and anyone with stock options is set to receive life-changing compensation
  • Work in a role with a high level of strategic impact, shaping the most important sourcing channels in the company’s largest market
  • Be part of an organisation already established internationally, with offices across Europe and the US
  • The sky is the limit for your own personal growth. Upwards mobility is a key feature of this business’s culture, with multiple examples of internal promotion in a rapidly scaling, entrepreneurial environment


If this role sounds like a strong fit for your background and skillset, please contact t.waterman@gs2partnership.com today.

title: Chief of Staff - PE backed
companyindustry: Hospitals and Health Care, Software Development, and Technology, Information and Media
descriptionhtml:
Our client, a PE-backed Healthcare business is shaping the transformation of the German healthcare sector by combining technology, data, and operational expertise. As they continue to scale, they are seeking a Chief of Staff to report into the Chief Product Technology Officer to act as an extension of the operations.


Key Responsibilities:


Operations

  • Manage meetings, agendas, decision papers, and follow-ups.


Prioritization and Governance

  • You run the prioritization process across all tech projects and subsidiaries – ensuring the right topics are advanced with the right level of priority.


OKRs and Reporting

  • You own the OKR framework in the tech area, consolidate status updates, and prepare board and private equity materials.


Budget Control

  • You track headcount and project budgets, identify deviations early, and prepare decision‑making documents.


Strategic Special Projects

  • You take on ad‑hoc projects at the direction of the CPTO – e.g., market research, organizational analyses, vendor evaluations.


Partner Engagement

  • You manage external partners in close alignment with the CPTO and ensure their delivery quality.


Experience Required:

  • 5+ years of experience in a similar role – ideally as a Strategy Manager, Chief of Staff, project lead, or in a top‑tier consultancy.
  • Tech affinity: You understand how software development and product development work.
  • Strong organizational skills.
  • Must be native/ business fluent in German and English.
  • Bonus: Experience in PE‑backed environments or in healthcare.

title: Customer Success Manager
descriptionhtml:
About Us


Supy is transforming the restaurant industry with a data-driven inventory management platform. Our solution enables multi-location restaurant groups to cut costs, reduce waste, and maximize profitability through real-time data, actionable insights, and time-saving automation. Join us in redefining restaurant operations through innovative technology and seamless customer experiences.


Role Overview


As a Customer Success Manager at Supy, you will be the key point of contact for our customers across Germany and the DACH region, owning the full post-sale lifecycle from onboarding to long-term growth. Your mission will be to ensure restaurant groups successfully adopt Supy and achieve measurable operational and financial impact.

This is a unique opportunity to shape how customer success is delivered in the region, with strong ownership and visibility in a fast-growing SaaS company.


Key Responsibilities


Customer Onboarding & Adoption

  • Guide restaurants through smooth onboarding, ensuring seamless integration and adoption of Supy’s solutions.
  • Train and support restaurant teams on platform features aligned with their operations.
  • Drive engagement across core modules including inventory, procurement, and cost control.


Account Management & Growth

  • Build and nurture long-term relationships with stakeholders across restaurant teams.
  • Act as a trusted advisor, providing insights to optimize operations and performance.
  • Identify opportunities for account expansion and support renewal processes.


Customer Success & Retention

  • Monitor account health, proactively identifying risks and ensuring high customer satisfaction.
  • Resolve issues promptly in line with SLA commitments.
  • Deliver tailored solutions by collaborating closely with internal teams.


Insights & Collaboration

  • Track and analyze KPIs, preparing reports that demonstrate value and ROI.
  • Relay customer feedback to product and internal teams to support continuous improvement.
  • Contribute to overall business growth through customer success outcomes.


Qualifications

  • 3–5+ years of experience in customer success or account management within B2B SaaS, hospitality tech, or POS/restaurant tech.
  • Strong understanding of restaurant operations, procurement, and cost management.
  • Ability to manage multiple customer accounts effectively.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to drive decisions and insights.
  • Familiarity with CRM and customer success tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, Gainsight).
  • Strong customer-centric mindset with a passion for helping clients succeed.
  • Full professional proficiency in German and English, both written and verbal.


Preferred Attributes

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, hospitality management, or a related field.
  • Previous experience as a cost controller in restaurants.
  • Experience in the F&B, hospitality, or restaurant tech space.
  • Basic understanding of SaaS platforms and APIs.
  • Excited to be part of a market launch and build something from the ground up.

description_html:
About Us


Supy is transforming the restaurant industry with a data-driven inventory management platform. Our solution enables multi-location restaurant groups to cut costs, reduce waste, and maximize profitability through real-time data, actionable insights, and time-saving automation. Join us in redefining restaurant operations through innovative technology and seamless customer experiences.


Role Overview


As a Customer Success Manager at Supy, you will be the key point of contact for our customers across Germany and the DACH region, owning the full post-sale lifecycle from onboarding to long-term growth. Your mission will be to ensure restaurant groups successfully adopt Supy and achieve measurable operational and financial impact.

This is a unique opportunity to shape how customer success is delivered in the region, with strong ownership and visibility in a fast-growing SaaS company.


Key Responsibilities


Customer Onboarding & Adoption

  • Guide restaurants through smooth onboarding, ensuring seamless integration and adoption of Supy’s solutions.
  • Train and support restaurant teams on platform features aligned with their operations.
  • Drive engagement across core modules including inventory, procurement, and cost control.


Account Management & Growth

  • Build and nurture long-term relationships with stakeholders across restaurant teams.
  • Act as a trusted advisor, providing insights to optimize operations and performance.
  • Identify opportunities for account expansion and support renewal processes.


Customer Success & Retention

  • Monitor account health, proactively identifying risks and ensuring high customer satisfaction.
  • Resolve issues promptly in line with SLA commitments.
  • Deliver tailored solutions by collaborating closely with internal teams.


Insights & Collaboration

  • Track and analyze KPIs, preparing reports that demonstrate value and ROI.
  • Relay customer feedback to product and internal teams to support continuous improvement.
  • Contribute to overall business growth through customer success outcomes.


Qualifications

  • 3–5+ years of experience in customer success or account management within B2B SaaS, hospitality tech, or POS/restaurant tech.
  • Strong understanding of restaurant operations, procurement, and cost management.
  • Ability to manage multiple customer accounts effectively.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to use data to drive decisions and insights.
  • Familiarity with CRM and customer success tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot, Gainsight).
  • Strong customer-centric mindset with a passion for helping clients succeed.
  • Full professional proficiency in German and English, both written and verbal.


Preferred Attributes

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, hospitality management, or a related field.
  • Previous experience as a cost controller in restaurants.
  • Experience in the F&B, hospitality, or restaurant tech space.
  • Basic understanding of SaaS platforms and APIs.
  • Excited to be part of a market launch and build something from the ground up.
reason: The role focuses on customer success and engagement rather than sales.

title: Partnerships Manager (m/w/d)
descriptionhtml:
Dein Impact

Du bist verantwortlich für die Monetarisierung unseres Medienportfolios (Social Media, Wertpapiersuche, Rechner, Podcasts, Newsletter). Dabei übernimmst du den Lead für alle Sponsoring- und Affiliate-Marketing-Aktivitäten bei Finanzfluss. Du unterstützt unsere Partner bei ihren digitalen Marketingkampagnen und stellst gleichzeitig sicher, dass wir unsere Umsatzziele erreichen und übertreffen. Dabei hast du ein gutes Gespür dafür, welche Kooperationen zu unserer Community passen und welche nicht.

Deine Aufgaben

  • Identifizierung neuer potenzieller Partner, die mit unseren Community- & Brand-Values übereinstimmen
  • Kooperationen entwickeln und verhandeln, bei denen Partner, Community und wir gewinnen
  • Optimierung und Pflege bestehender Geschäftsbeziehungen sowie nachhaltige Absicherung unserer Partnerverträge
  • Erster Ansprechpartner für jegliche Werbekooperationen und Repräsentation von Finanzfluss bei Branchenevents
  • Positionierung und Präsentation unseres Medienportfolios und Werbeinventars
  • Du arbeitest eng mit Content, Product und unseren Partnern zusammen, damit Kooperationen reibungslos laufen

Deine Skills

  • Du hast mindestens 2 Jahre Berufserfahrung im Bereich Sales/Partnerships, Affiliate Marketing oder Account Management (idealerweise bist du im Finanzbereich bereits gut vernetzt)
  • Ausgezeichnete Deutsch- und gute Englischkenntnisse in Wort und Schrift
  • Du bist proaktiv und hast eine ausgeprägte Kommunikationsfähigkeit
  • Feingefühl und Positivität sind ein wesentlicher Bestandteil deines Verhandlungsgeschicks
  • Du kennst die Online-Marketing-Welt und weißt, welche Kennzahlen bei digitalen Kampagnen wirklich zählen.
  • Analytisches und strategisches Denken liegen dir
  • Auf dich ist Verlass und du hältst Deadlines und Absprachen ein

Wir bieten dir

  • Ein kompetitives Vergütungspaket inklusive Beteiligung am Unternehmenserfolg (VSOP)
  • Einen Boost für deine Growth Journey: Entwickle dich in einem stark wachsenden, profitablen Start-up zur besten Version deiner selbst.
  • Modernste Tools & Arbeitsumgebung: Wähle deinen bevorzugten Tech Stack und arbeite in unserem Office im Herzen von Kreuzberg, inklusive flexibler Homeoffice-Regelungen.
  • Gestaltungsspielraum auf höchstem Niveau: Treibe die Strategie von Finanzfluss aktiv mit voran und bring deine eigenen Ideen direkt ein
  • Ein ambitioniertes Team & eine einzigartige Community

Über uns

Bei uns zählst du. Bei Finanzfluss feiern wir Vielfalt und sind überzeugt, dass unterschiedliche Perspektiven, Erfahrungen und Hintergründe uns als Team stärker machen. Deshalb heißen wir Bewerbungen unabhängig von Geschlecht, Alter, Herkunft, Religion, sexueller Orientierung, körperlichen Fähigkeiten oder Familienstand herzlich willkommen. Wenn du dich von dieser Stelle angesprochen fühlst, freuen wir uns darauf, dich kennenzulernen.

title: Head of Strategic Partnerships (m/w/d) COO Track
descriptionhtml:
Join PsyCurio and help shape the future of digital health and mental well-being. We develop evidence-based digital solutions that measurably improve mental performance and sustainable well-being.


With proven market success in Germany and Switzerland, partnerships with health insurers, and collaborations with leading international research institutions, we are entering our next growth phase. Supported by the German Accelerator program in the US, we are expanding globally — and are looking for a key person to drive this journey.


This role offers a clear path toward a COO position, ideal for entrepreneurial individuals who want to actively shape a company rather than just work within it.


Tasks

  • Build, develop, and manage strategic partnerships (e.g., health insurers, digital platforms, international partners)


Identify and unlock new growth and collaboration opportunities


Support fundraising activities and investor relations


Develop and execute go-to-market strategies together with partners


Drive and expand business development initiatives


Collaborate closely with product, operations, and the founding team


Contribute to international expansion (DACH, US, global)


Help shape the long-term strategic direction of the company


Requirements

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong sense of ownership and responsibility


Experience or strong interest in business development, partnerships, or strategy


Ability to build and maintain long-term relationships with stakeholders


Strong analytical and structured working style


Excellent communication and negotiation skills


Willingness and potential to take on leadership responsibility early


Fluent in English and German


Passion for digital health, psychology, or healthcare innovation


Based in Berlin or willing to relocate (remote within Germany possible)You are self-reliant and responsible


Benefits

COO Track: Clear development path toward an operational leadership role or co-founder position

Equity participation: Long-term upside and ownership in the company

High impact: Direct influence on strategy, partnerships, and growth from day one

Startup environment: Fast-paced, dynamic, and mission-driven team

Learning & exposure: Insights into fundraising, international scaling, and leadership

Flexibility: Remote work options and flexible working hours

Flat hierarchies: Short decision-making paths and real ownership

Creative freedom: Space to bring in your own ideas and shape processes


We encourage young MBA graduates/graduates to apply for this position as well as experienced leaders!

description_html:
Join PsyCurio and help shape the future of digital health and mental well-being. We develop evidence-based digital solutions that measurably improve mental performance and sustainable well-being.


With proven market success in Germany and Switzerland, partnerships with health insurers, and collaborations with leading international research institutions, we are entering our next growth phase. Supported by the German Accelerator program in the US, we are expanding globally — and are looking for a key person to drive this journey.


This role offers a clear path toward a COO position, ideal for entrepreneurial individuals who want to actively shape a company rather than just work within it.


Tasks

  • Build, develop, and manage strategic partnerships (e.g., health insurers, digital platforms, international partners)


Identify and unlock new growth and collaboration opportunities


Support fundraising activities and investor relations


Develop and execute go-to-market strategies together with partners


Drive and expand business development initiatives


Collaborate closely with product, operations, and the founding team


Contribute to international expansion (DACH, US, global)


Help shape the long-term strategic direction of the company


Requirements

  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a strong sense of ownership and responsibility


Experience or strong interest in business development, partnerships, or strategy


Ability to build and maintain long-term relationships with stakeholders


Strong analytical and structured working style


Excellent communication and negotiation skills


Willingness and potential to take on leadership responsibility early


Fluent in English and German


Passion for digital health, psychology, or healthcare innovation


Based in Berlin or willing to relocate (remote within Germany possible)You are self-reliant and responsible


Benefits

COO Track: Clear development path toward an operational leadership role or co-founder position

Equity participation: Long-term upside and ownership in the company

High impact: Direct influence on strategy, partnerships, and growth from day one

Startup environment: Fast-paced, dynamic, and mission-driven team

Learning & exposure: Insights into fundraising, international scaling, and leadership

Flexibility: Remote work options and flexible working hours

Flat hierarchies: Short decision-making paths and real ownership

Creative freedom: Space to bring in your own ideas and shape processes


We encourage young MBA graduates/graduates to apply for this position as well as experienced leaders!

title: Strategic Partnerships Manager UK (f/m/d)
companyindustry: Construction, Software Development, and IT Services and IT Consulting
descriptionhtml:
Total Compensation Range: €56,300 - €85,700

The Position

The UK is one of Hive's most important and fastest-growing markets, and this role sits right at the heart of it. As Strategic Partnerships Manager UK, you will own Hive's delivery, freight, and fulfilment partner portfolio in the UK end-to-end, working out of our Berlin HQ. This is a high-ownership, high-visibility role where you will be the face of Operations for the UK market, both internally and externally. As part of this role, you will:

  • Own the full relationship with Hive's UK delivery, freight, and fulfilment partners from smaller day-to-day projects through to long-term strategy
  • Negotiate carrier and partner contracts, driving cost efficiency while maintaining high service levels
  • Track and act on partner performance data to identify improvements, resolve issues, and keep operations running smoothly
  • Run regular business reviews and operational check-ins with partners, ensuring accountability on both sides
  • Develop the UK partner portfolio in line with Hive's growth ambitions, making our offering the most attractive for D2C brands in the market
  • Work closely with Growth, Account Management, and Merchant Care to translate market needs into partner requirements

Your Profile

We know, sometimes you can't tick every box. We would still love to hear from you if you think you're a good fit!

  • You have 3-5 years of experience in operations, logistics, supply chain, consulting, or a fast-growth startup, and you have worked in environments where ownership and pace matter
  • You have a track record of managing commercial relationships end-to-end with carriers, 3PLs, freight or fulfilment providers, or other external partners
  • You work with a hands-on, entrepreneurial mentality, take initiative, move fast, and drive impact without waiting to be told
  • You are analytically strong and confident using data to drive negotiations and performance conversations
  • You have excellent communication skills and fluency in English; additional languages such as German are a plus
  • You thrive in fast-scaling, ambiguous environments and act like the owner of your domain

Our offering

  • Build something that matters: Hive is redefining how commerce operations work in a €200B+ market. We already reach tens of millions of consumers every year and empower over 500 independent brands to grow and thrive - and we’re just getting started.
  • Work with exceptional people: Join a high-performing, ambitious team, including many from world-class companies such as McKinsey, Amazon, Shopify, Google, TikTok, and Trade Republic.
  • Grow with Hive: From day one, you’ll take ownership of ambitious projects and grow alongside Hive as we scale. We believe in meritocracy - your impact drives your progression, not your past titles.
  • Competitive compensation: We offer market-competitive salaries and an equity incentive plan (EIP) for all full-time team members.
  • Tools to thrive: Choose the setup that helps you do your best work - a MacBook (or laptop of your choice), plus accessories like an extra monitor or headphones to match your working style.
  • Well-being matters: Enjoy 30 vacation days per year, plus a paid 20-day sabbatical every three years to recharge and reflect.
  • Flexibility and connection: Benefit from flexible working hours, free drinks and snacks at our offices, and regular team events - including company-wide offsites that bring all of Hive together.

About Us

We're revolutionizing e-commerce operations.

At Hive, we empower brands to excel in the digital commerce era through our innovative operations platform. By combining cutting-edge technology with a curated network of top-tier operations partners, we deliver measurable results.

Our comprehensive platform streamlines the entire operational chain through a single, intuitive interface. Since our founding in 2020, we've rapidly grown to become one of Europe's leading operations platform, partnering with hundreds of innovative brands. With strategic locations in Berlin, Paris, Milan, Madrid, London, and Amsterdam. Backed by prestigious investors including Tiger Global, Earlybird, and Picus Capital, we're scaling our impact across Europe.

Diversity and inclusion are core to our success. We actively cultivate an environment where every team member, regardless of background, can thrive. We welcome talent from all walks of life, regardless of religion, ethnicity, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, or disability. At Hive, authenticity and professional growth go hand in hand.

title: Strategic Partner Manager, DACH, News, YouTube
companyindustry: Information Services and Technology, Information and Internet
descriptionhtml:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Berlin, Germany; Munich, Germany.Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 4 years of experience in business development, partnerships, management consulting, or investment banking.
  • Experience working with C-level executives and cross-functionally across all levels of management.
  • Experience managing agreements or partnerships.
  • Experience working in the News and Publishing industry.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Experience managing partnerships, with a focus on tracking performance metrics, forecasting outcomes, and maintaining a positive partner relationship.
  • Experience working with Product, Legal, and Communications teams to execute joint initiatives and navigate internal landscapes.
  • Understanding of the regional news ecosystem and market trends to inform decision-making and product development.
  • Ability to plan and lead prioritized projects using formal methodologies to deliver results within strict timelines.
  • Ability to resolve partner escalations and advocate for partner needs while maintaining productive, professional relationships.

About the jobAs a part of Strategic Partner Development Manager in Germany, you will cultivate and manage high-impact partnerships within the News and Journalism ecosystem. In this role, you will be instrumental in building and strengthening relationships with traditional broadcasters, digital-first news publishers, and journalists. You will serve as a lead consultant on their YouTube content strategy, helping them navigate the evolving media landscape and meticulously tracking impact to drive mutual, sustainable success.

In this role, you will leverage your expertise in relationship building, negotiation, project management, and cross-functional collaboration to contribute to the strategic development of YouTube's partnerships in Germany. You will grow in a fast-paced environment, demonstrating a strong understanding of Google's internal processes and products. Your contributions will not only drive business objectives but also enhance company culture.At YouTube, we believe that everyone deserves to have a voice, and that the world is a better place when we share, and build community through our stories. We work together to give everyone the power to share their story, explore what they love, and connect with one another in the process. Working at the intersection of technology and boundless creativity, we move at the speed of culture with a shared goal to show people the world. We explore new ideas, solve real problems, and have fun — and we do it all together.

Responsibilities

  • Act as the primary lead for major DACH news publishers, developing data-driven business plans and content strategies to drive growth and platform advocacy.
  • Drive adoption of YouTube news products and features by identifying partners for alpha/beta testing and synthesizing feedback to influence global products and GTM roadmaps.
  • Resolve partner escalations and policy/copyright issues by collaborating with Legal, Trust and Safety, and Operations teams to ensure ecosystem health.
  • Represent YouTube News externally at industry events and workshops, while surfacing unique cross-Google opportunities to deepen partner relationships.
  • Enhance the DACH partner landscape through pitch best practices while actively contributing to Google’s internal culture and cross-functional synergy with Sales, Marketing, and Communications.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .

description_html:
Note: By applying to this position you will have an opportunity to share your preferred working location from the following: Berlin, Germany; Munich, Germany.Minimum qualifications:

  • Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
  • 4 years of experience in business development, partnerships, management consulting, or investment banking.
  • Experience working with C-level executives and cross-functionally across all levels of management.
  • Experience managing agreements or partnerships.
  • Experience working in the News and Publishing industry.

Preferred qualifications:

  • Experience managing partnerships, with a focus on tracking performance metrics, forecasting outcomes, and maintaining a positive partner relationship.
  • Experience working with Product, Legal, and Communications teams to execute joint initiatives and navigate internal landscapes.
  • Understanding of the regional news ecosystem and market trends to inform decision-making and product development.
  • Ability to plan and lead prioritized projects using formal methodologies to deliver results within strict timelines.
  • Ability to resolve partner escalations and advocate for partner needs while maintaining productive, professional relationships.

About the jobAs a part of Strategic Partner Development Manager in Germany, you will cultivate and manage high-impact partnerships within the News and Journalism ecosystem. In this role, you will be instrumental in building and strengthening relationships with traditional broadcasters, digital-first news publishers, and journalists. You will serve as a lead consultant on their YouTube content strategy, helping them navigate the evolving media landscape and meticulously tracking impact to drive mutual, sustainable success.

In this role, you will leverage your expertise in relationship building, negotiation, project management, and cross-functional collaboration to contribute to the strategic development of YouTube's partnerships in Germany. You will grow in a fast-paced environment, demonstrating a strong understanding of Google's internal processes and products. Your contributions will not only drive business objectives but also enhance company culture.At YouTube, we believe that everyone deserves to have a voice, and that the world is a better place when we share, and build community through our stories. We work together to give everyone the power to share their story, explore what they love, and connect with one another in the process. Working at the intersection of technology and boundless creativity, we move at the speed of culture with a shared goal to show people the world. We explore new ideas, solve real problems, and have fun — and we do it all together.

Responsibilities

  • Act as the primary lead for major DACH news publishers, developing data-driven business plans and content strategies to drive growth and platform advocacy.
  • Drive adoption of YouTube news products and features by identifying partners for alpha/beta testing and synthesizing feedback to influence global products and GTM roadmaps.
  • Resolve partner escalations and policy/copyright issues by collaborating with Legal, Trust and Safety, and Operations teams to ensure ecosystem health.
  • Represent YouTube News externally at industry events and workshops, while surfacing unique cross-Google opportunities to deepen partner relationships.
  • Enhance the DACH partner landscape through pitch best practices while actively contributing to Google’s internal culture and cross-functional synergy with Sales, Marketing, and Communications.


Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form .
